regulationName: Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule (16 CFR Part 435)
chineseTitle: 联邦邮购/网购/电话订购商品规则（30天发货规则）
englishTitle: FTC Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule ("30-Day Rule")
agency: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
applicabilityThreshold: Any seller of merchandise ordered by mail, internet, or telephone (含通过App下单的商品/预付订单).
keyRequirements: Sellers must ship mail/internet/phone orders within the advertised time or, if none is stated, within 30 days, and must offer to cancel with a prompt refund if that deadline cannot be met.
complaintEnforcementRecord: FTC has brought numerous 30-Day Rule actions; e.g., FTC penalties against online sellers for COVID-era PPE shipping delays (2020-2021) for failing to give timely delay/cancel notices.
recommendedAction: 为应用/网店商品订单设定发货时限与延迟通知/退款流程 — Build shipping-deadline tracking and automated delay/refund notices into e-commerce fulfillment.
chineseSummary: 该规则要求卖方在广告承诺的时间内发货，若未注明则须在30天内发货。无法按时发货时，必须通知消费者并提供取消订单及及时退款的选项。1975年发布，1993年扩展至电话订单，2014年修订并更名以涵盖互联网订单（2014年修订自2014年12月8日生效）。
englishSummary: The rule requires sellers to ship merchandise ordered by mail, internet, or telephone within the time stated, or within 30 days if no time is stated. If the seller cannot meet the deadline, it must notify the buyer and offer to cancel the order with a prompt refund. Originally promulgated in 1975; expanded to telephone orders in 1993; amended and renamed in 2014 (the 2014 amendments effective Dec 8, 2014) to expressly cover internet orders.

regulationName: FTC Cooling-Off Rule (16 CFR Part 429)
chineseTitle: 联邦三日冷静期规则（上门/非营业场所销售）
englishTitle: FTC Cooling-Off Rule — 3-Day Right to Cancel
agency: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
applicabilityThreshold: Door-to-door consumer sales of $25+ at the buyer's home, or $130+ at temporary locations — 不适用于固定门店内的常规零售.
keyRequirements: For consumer sales of $25+ at the buyer's home (or $130+ at a temporary location away from the seller's permanent place of business), the seller must give written notice of the buyer's right to cancel within three business days.
recommendedAction: 若开展线下临时/活动销售，准备合规的取消通知模板 — If running off-site/pop-up sales above the threshold, prepare compliant cancellation-notice forms.
chineseSummary: 对于在消费者住所进行的25美元以上销售，或在卖方非固定营业场所（如集市、临时摊位、活动现场）进行的130美元以上销售，卖方必须以书面形式告知消费者享有三个工作日内取消交易并获退款的权利。1974年生效。
englishSummary: For consumer sales of $25 or more made at a buyer's home, or $130 or more at temporary locations away from the seller's permanent place of business (such as fairs, pop-ups, or event tables), the seller must give the buyer written notice of a three-business-day right to cancel and refund. Effective 1974.

regulationName: FTC Act Unfair or Deceptive Acts or Practices — Advertising Substantiation Doctrine
chineseTitle: 联邦广告实证原则（不公平/欺骗性行为）
englishTitle: FTC Advertising Substantiation Doctrine (UDAP under FTC Act §5)
agency: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
applicabilityThreshold: Any business making objective advertising claims to consumers (适用于所有面向消费者的广告宣称).
keyRequirements: Advertisers must possess a reasonable basis (competent and reliable evidence) substantiating objective product claims before they are disseminated.
complaintEnforcementRecord: FTC routinely sues for unsubstantiated claims; e.g., FTC v. multiple beverage/supplement marketers for unsupported health/energy claims.
recommendedAction: 建立广告宣称的证据存档与法务审核流程 — Maintain a claims-substantiation file and legal review for all marketing claims.
chineseSummary: FTC的广告实证政策声明（1983年3月11日发布）要求广告主在发布任何客观产品宣称（如健康、营养、来源、品质宣称）之前，必须持有合理依据，即有效可靠的证据支撑。缺乏实证的宣称即构成FTC法案第5条下的欺骗行为。
englishSummary: The FTC's Policy Statement Regarding Advertising Substantiation (issued March 11, 1983) requires advertisers to have a reasonable basis (competent and reliable evidence) for objective claims before they are made. Unsubstantiated claims—about health, nutrition, sourcing, or quality—are deceptive under Section 5 of the FTC Act.

regulationName: FTC Guides Concerning the Use of Endorsements and Testimonials in Advertising (16 CFR Part 255)
chineseTitle: 联邦广告代言与推荐指南（2023年修订）
englishTitle: FTC Endorsement Guides (16 CFR Part 255) — 2023 Revision
agency: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
applicabilityThreshold: Any advertiser using endorsements, testimonials, or influencers (适用于所有使用代言/网红/推荐的广告主).
keyRequirements: Any material connection between a brand and an endorser (free product, payment, affiliate commissions, employment) must be clearly and conspicuously disclosed in advertising, including by influencers.
complaintEnforcementRecord: Nov 2023: FTC sent warning letters to two trade associations and 12 health influencers for inadequate disclosures — its first action against food/beverage trade groups for social media advertising.
recommendedAction: 在网红合同中强制要求清晰显著的合作披露并监控合规 — Mandate clear #ad disclosures in influencer contracts and monitor posts for compliance.
chineseSummary: FTC代言指南于2023年6月29日宣布重大修订（自2009年以来首次），并于2023年7月26日刊登于《联邦公报》，明确品牌与代言人之间的任何实质性关联（免费产品、付费合作、佣金、雇佣关系）必须在广告中清晰显著披露。品牌可能因代言人未披露而承担责任。涵盖社交媒体网红和虚假评价。
englishSummary: The FTC's Endorsement Guides, substantially revised and announced June 29, 2023 (first update since 2009) and published in the Federal Register July 26, 2023, clarify that any material connection between a brand and an endorser must be clearly and conspicuously disclosed. Brands can be liable for an influencer's failure to disclose, even without explicit instruction. The guides now address social media influencers and fake reviews.

regulationName: FTC Rule on the Use of Consumer Reviews and Testimonials (16 CFR Part 465)
chineseTitle: 联邦消费者评价与推荐规则（禁止虚假评价）
englishTitle: FTC Rule on Consumer Reviews and Testimonials (16 CFR Part 465)
agency: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
applicabilityThreshold: Any business that solicits, displays, or manages consumer reviews/testimonials (适用于所有使用消费者评价的企业).
keyRequirements: Prohibits creating, buying, or selling fake/AI-generated reviews and testimonials, undisclosed insider reviews, and suppression or distortion of genuine consumer reviews.
complaintEnforcementRecord: FTC has pursued review-related actions (e.g., 2022 Fashion Nova action over review suppression) that prefigured this rule.
recommendedAction: 审查评价营销实践，禁止虚假/内部评价并停止删评 — Audit review-marketing practices; prohibit fake/insider reviews and review suppression.
chineseSummary: FTC于2024年8月14日最终确定该规则（2024年10月21日生效），禁止企业撰写、购买或销售虚假或AI生成的评价和推荐、未披露的内部人员评价，以及压制或扭曲真实评价。违规每次可处最高约51,744美元民事罚款。
englishSummary: Finalized August 14, 2024 and effective October 21, 2024, this rule bans creating, purchasing, or selling fake or AI-generated reviews, undisclosed insider reviews, and the suppression or distortion of genuine consumer reviews. Violations can carry civil penalties of up to $51,744 each.

regulationName: Electronic Fund Transfer Act / Regulation E — Gift Card Rules (CARD Act; 12 CFR 1005.20)
chineseTitle: 联邦礼品卡规则（CARD法案 / Reg E 第1005.20条）
englishTitle: Federal Gift Card Rules — CARD Act / Regulation E (12 CFR 1005.20)
agency: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
applicabilityThreshold: Issuers/sellers of gift certificates, store gift cards, or general-use prepaid cards (适用于发行/销售礼品卡或储值卡的商家).
keyRequirements: Gift cards/certificates may not expire sooner than five years from issuance or last load, dormancy/inactivity fees are barred unless inactive 12+ months (then max one per month), and all fee and expiration terms must be clearly and conspicuously disclosed before purchase.
recommendedAction: 审核礼品卡/储值条款符合5年到期与费用披露规则 — Confirm gift-card/stored-value terms meet 5-year expiry and fee-disclosure rules.
chineseSummary: 依据2009年CARD法案并由Reg E（第1005.20条）实施，礼品卡资金自发行或最后充值起五年内不得到期；除非闲置满12个月否则不得收取不活动费（之后每月最多一次）。所有费用和到期条款须在购买前清晰显著披露。2010年生效，监管权现属CFPB。
englishSummary: Under the 2009 CARD Act, implemented by Regulation E §1005.20, gift card funds cannot expire less than five years from issuance or last load, and dormancy/inactivity fees are prohibited unless the card has been inactive 12+ months (then no more than one per month). All fee and expiration terms must be clearly and conspicuously disclosed before purchase. Effective 2010; now administered by the CFPB.

regulationName: Restore Online Shoppers' Confidence Act (ROSCA, 15 U.S.C. 8401-8405) — Online Negative Option / Auto-Renewal
chineseTitle: 联邦恢复网购信心法案（ROSCA）— 在线自动续订/订阅
englishTitle: Restore Online Shoppers' Confidence Act (ROSCA) — Auto-Renewal & Subscriptions
agency: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
applicabilityThreshold: Sellers offering online negative-option, auto-renewal, or subscription features to consumers (适用于自动续订/订阅类产品).
keyRequirements: For online negative-option/subscription offers, sellers must clearly and conspicuously disclose all material terms, obtain the consumer's express informed consent before charging, and provide a simple mechanism to stop recurring charges.
complaintEnforcementRecord: FTC has obtained large ROSCA settlements (e.g., Amazon Prime litigation 2023, Vonage $100M 2022) over difficult cancellation and undisclosed recurring charges.
recommendedAction: 为订阅/会员产品配置清晰条款披露、明确同意与一键取消 — Implement clear disclosure, affirmative consent, and easy cancellation for any subscription/membership.
chineseSummary: ROSCA（2010年12月29日签署生效）禁止在线卖方在未清晰显著披露条款、未获得消费者明确知情同意前对负面选项/订阅收费，并要求提供简便的取消方式。FTC据此（结合FTC法案第5条）规制咖啡订阅、会员制等自动续费产品。注意：FTC 2024年对《负面选项规则》（16 CFR 425，即"一键取消"）的修订已于2025年7月被第八巡回法院以程序瑕疵为由撤销，故ROSCA与FTC法案第5条仍是现行主要执法依据。
englishSummary: ROSCA (signed Dec 29, 2010) prohibits online sellers from charging consumers for negative-option/subscription features without clearly and conspicuously disclosing terms, obtaining express informed consent, and providing a simple mechanism to stop recurring charges. The FTC uses ROSCA and Section 5 to police coffee subscriptions, memberships, and recurring-charge offers. Note: the FTC's 2024 amendments to the separate Negative Option Rule (16 CFR Part 425, the "Click-to-Cancel" rule) were vacated by the Eighth Circuit in July 2025 on procedural grounds, so ROSCA and FTC Act §5 remain the operative federal authority.

regulationName: FTC Standards for Safeguarding Customer Information (Safeguards Rule, 16 CFR Part 314) — 2023 Breach-Notification Amendment
chineseTitle: 联邦客户信息保护规则（保障规则）— 2023年数据泄露通报修订
englishTitle: FTC Safeguards Rule (16 CFR Part 314) — 2023 Breach Notification Amendment
agency: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
applicabilityThreshold: Only 'financial institutions' under GLBA (非银行金融机构) — a pure coffee retailer is generally not covered unless it extends/manages consumer credit.
keyRequirements: Covered non-banking financial institutions must maintain a written information security program and, under the 2023 amendment, notify the FTC within 30 days of a security event involving the unencrypted information of 500+ consumers.
recommendedAction: 评估是否构成GLBA'金融机构'；若是，部署安全计划与30天泄露通报流程 — Assess whether any finance arm is a covered 'financial institution'; if so, implement the program and 30-day breach-notice process.
chineseSummary: GLBA下的FTC保障规则要求受监管的非银行金融机构建立书面信息安全计划。2023年修订（2024年5月13日生效）新增数据泄露通报义务：涉及500名以上消费者未加密信息的安全事件须在发现后30天内通报FTC。是否适用取决于商家是否构成'金融机构'（如自营信贷/分期）。
englishSummary: The GLBA-based FTC Safeguards Rule requires covered non-banking financial institutions to maintain a written information security program. A 2023 amendment, effective May 13, 2024, adds a duty to notify the FTC within 30 days of a security event involving the unencrypted information of 500 or more consumers. Applicability depends on whether the business qualifies as a covered 'financial institution.'

regulationName: Americans with Disabilities Act, Title III — Public Accommodations & 2010 ADA Standards for Accessible Design (28 CFR Part 36)
chineseTitle: 《美国残疾人法》第三章 — 公共设施无障碍（2010无障碍设计标准）
englishTitle: ADA Title III — Public Accommodations & 2010 Accessible Design Standards (28 CFR Part 36)
agency: U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ), Civil Rights Division
applicabilityThreshold: All places of public accommodation open to the public (适用于所有对公众开放的门店).
keyRequirements: Places of public accommodation, including restaurants and cafés, must remove architectural barriers where readily achievable and ensure new construction/alterations comply with the 2010 ADA Standards for Accessible Design.
complaintEnforcementRecord: ADA Title III physical-access suits against restaurant/café chains are common; DOJ and private plaintiffs regularly enforce barrier-removal and counter-height requirements.
recommendedAction: 对每家门店进行ADA物理无障碍审计（柜台高度、入口、卫生间、通道） — Conduct an ADA physical-accessibility audit (counters, entrances, restrooms, routes) for every location.
chineseSummary: ADA第三章禁止在公共设施（包括咖啡馆、餐厅）中基于残疾的歧视，要求在'易于实现'的范围内拆除建筑障碍，并要求新建/改建符合2010无障碍设计标准（如柜台高度、入口坡道、无障碍卫生间、通道宽度）。2010标准自2012年3月15日起强制适用于新建/改建。
englishSummary: ADA Title III bars disability discrimination in places of public accommodation, including cafés and restaurants, requiring removal of architectural barriers where readily achievable and full compliance with the 2010 ADA Standards for Accessible Design in new construction and alterations (e.g., counter heights, entrance ramps, accessible restrooms, route widths). The 2010 Standards became mandatory for new/altered facilities on March 15, 2012.

regulationName: NIST Handbook 130 — Uniform Regulation for the Method of Sale of Commodities (Model Weights & Measures Law)
chineseTitle: NIST第130号手册 — 商品销售方式统一法规（度量衡示范法）
englishTitle: NIST Handbook 130 — Uniform Method of Sale & Weights and Measures Model Law
agency: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), Office of Weights and Measures
applicabilityThreshold: Commodities sold by weight, measure, or count (model law applied via individual state adoption — 通过各州采纳生效).
keyRequirements: Provides model uniform laws/regulations—adopted by most states—governing net-quantity declarations, unit pricing, and the method of sale of commodities, ensuring quantities and prices are accurately represented to consumers.
recommendedAction: 确保按重量/容量销售的包装商品标示准确净含量并符合所在州度量衡法 — Ensure packaged/by-weight products carry accurate net-quantity labels per each state's weights-and-measures adoption.
chineseSummary: NIST第130号手册是联邦发布的度量衡示范法规汇编（含'商品销售方式统一法规'与'度量衡统一法'），被绝大多数州采纳。它规定净含量标示、单位定价、按重量/容量销售商品的方式，确保向消费者准确表示数量和价格。NIST按年度更新该手册（2024版后已发布2025版）；适用以各州实际采纳为准。
englishSummary: NIST Handbook 130 is the federally published compilation of model weights-and-measures laws and regulations (including the Uniform Method of Sale of Commodities Regulation and Uniform Weights and Measures Law) adopted in some form by most states. It governs net-quantity declarations, unit pricing, and how commodities sold by weight/volume must be represented. NIST updates it annually (a 2025 edition has since been published after the 2024 edition); legal force comes only through each state's own adoption.

regulationName: FTC Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees ("Junk Fees Rule," 16 CFR Part 464)
chineseTitle: 联邦不公平/欺骗性费用规则（'垃圾费用'规则）
englishTitle: FTC Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees ("Junk Fees Rule," 16 CFR Part 464)
agency: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
applicabilityThreshold: Live-event ticketing and short-term lodging only (现行最终规则不适用于餐饮/咖啡馆).
keyRequirements: For live-event tickets and short-term lodging, sellers must disclose the all-in "Total Price" including mandatory fees up front and may not misrepresent fees; the final rule does NOT cover restaurants or food service.
recommendedAction: 监测FTC全包价执法趋势；自愿在菜单/App清晰披露任何附加费 — Monitor FTC all-in-pricing enforcement; voluntarily disclose any surcharges/fees clearly on menus/app.
chineseSummary: FTC于2024年12月最终确定该规则（2025年5月12日生效），要求现场活动门票和短期住宿的卖方在前期清晰披露包含强制性费用的'总价'，并禁止误导性费用表述。最终版范围大幅收窄，仅限票务与短租住宿，明确不涵盖餐饮业，故对咖啡馆仅为参考。
englishSummary: Finalized in December 2024 and effective May 12, 2025, this rule requires sellers of live-event tickets and short-term lodging to disclose an all-in "Total Price" including mandatory fees up front and bars fee misrepresentation. The final rule was narrowed substantially and does not cover restaurants or food service, making it informational context for cafés.

regulationName: FTC Commercial Surveillance and Data Security Rulemaking (ANPR, 2022 — pending/inactive)
chineseTitle: 联邦商业监控与数据安全立法（2022预先通知，待定/搁置）
englishTitle: FTC Commercial Surveillance & Data Security Rulemaking (ANPR — Pending/Inactive)
agency: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
applicabilityThreshold: Scope undefined (pending); would likely reach businesses collecting consumer data via apps/loyalty programs (范围待定).
keyRequirements: Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking exploring potential federal rules on how companies collect, use, retain, share, secure, and monetize consumer data, including automated decision-making and consumer consent.
recommendedAction: 跟踪该立法进展，提前梳理App/会员数据收集与同意机制 — Track the rulemaking and proactively map app/loyalty data collection and consent practices.
chineseSummary: FTC于2022年8月发布预先拟议规则通知（ANPR），探讨是否针对企业收集、使用、保留、共享、保护和变现消费者数据的方式（含自动化决策与同意机制）制定联邦规则。该立法仍处于早期阶段，在现任FTC（Ferguson主席）领导下基本被搁置/不再推进，尚无生效日期。
englishSummary: In August 2022 the FTC issued an Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (ANPR) exploring potential federal rules on commercial surveillance—how companies collect, use, retain, share, secure, and monetize consumer data, including automated decision-making and consent. It remains at an early stage and has effectively been shelved/deprioritized under the current FTC (Chairman Ferguson); no proposed rule or effective date exists.

regulationName: Restaurant Surcharges Rule (6 RCNY § 5-59)
chineseTitle: 纽约市餐厅附加费与强制小费披露规则
englishTitle: NYC Restaurant Surcharges & Mandatory Gratuity Disclosure Rule
agency: NYC Department of Consumer and Worker Protection (DCWP)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于所有纽约市售卖即食食品/饮料的餐饮场所 / Applies to all NYC food-service establishments selling prepared food or beverages.
keyRequirements: Restaurants may not add a surcharge on top of listed menu prices, and any bona fide service charge or mandatory gratuity must be conspicuously disclosed to the consumer before food or beverage is ordered (not hidden on the back of a menu or out of view).
complaintEnforcementRecord: DCWP publishes an inspection checklist specifically for 'Restaurant Surcharges and Mandatory Gratuities' and inspects/fines establishments that add prohibited surcharges or fail to disclose service charges before ordering.
recommendedAction: 审查菜单、点单屏与收据，删除任何在已列价格之上的附加费；如保留服务费，须在点单前显著披露 / Audit menu boards, kiosks and receipts to remove any surcharge over posted prices; disclose any retained service fee before ordering.
chineseSummary: 纽约市消费者保护规则禁止餐厅在已列出的菜单价格之上加收附加费（surcharge）。如收取正当的服务费或对特定情形的强制小费，必须在顾客点单前以显著方式披露，不得隐藏于菜单背面或顾客看不到的位置。该规则由 DCWP 依据《消费者保护法》（NYC 行政法典 §20-700）执行。
englishSummary: NYC's consumer-protection rule prohibits restaurants from imposing a surcharge over and above posted menu prices. Bona fide service charges and mandatory gratuities are permitted only if conspicuously disclosed before the consumer orders, and disclosures hidden on the back of a menu or out of view are violations. DCWP enforces the rule under the Consumer Protection Law (Admin. Code § 20-700).

regulationName: Prohibition of Cashless Establishments (Local Law 34 of 2020; NYC Admin. Code § 20-840)
chineseTitle: 纽约市禁止无现金商户法
englishTitle: NYC Prohibition of Cashless Establishments
agency: NYC Department of Consumer and Worker Protection (DCWP)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于纽约市内所有零售商户和食品店（含咖啡店）/ Applies to all NYC retail establishments and food stores, including coffee shops.
keyRequirements: Food stores and retail establishments must accept cash payments and may not charge cash-paying customers a higher price than card-paying customers, subject to limited exceptions (e.g., on-site cash-to-prepaid-card machine; bills over $20; remote orders).
complaintEnforcementRecord: DCWP settled with Van Leeuwen Ice Cream over cashless-establishment violations (2022), under which the company agreed to accept cash at all NYC locations and pay penalties.
recommendedAction: 确保每家纽约市门店有可接受现金的支付渠道，并培训员工不得拒收现金 / Ensure every NYC store has a staffed cash-acceptance option and train staff never to refuse cash.
chineseSummary: 纽约地方法 34 号（行政法典 §20-840）要求食品店和零售商户必须接受现金付款，且不得对以现金付款的顾客收取高于刷卡顾客的价格。例外包括：店内提供可将现金转为预付卡的机器、可拒收面额超过 20 美元的钞票、以及完全在线/电话/邮购的交易。首次违规罚款最高 1,000 美元，其后每次最高 1,500 美元。
englishSummary: Local Law 34 of 2020 (Admin. Code § 20-840) requires food stores and retail establishments to accept cash and bars charging cash customers more than card customers. Narrow exceptions include having an on-premises cash-to-prepaid-card machine, refusing bills over $20, and fully remote (online/phone/mail) orders. Penalties are up to $1,000 for a first violation and up to $1,500 for each subsequent violation, enforced by DCWP.

regulationName: Bag Waste Reduction Act (ECL Art. 27, Title 28) and NYC 5-Cent Paper Carryout Bag Fee
chineseTitle: 纽约州减少购物袋废弃物法及纽约市纸袋5美分收费
englishTitle: NY Bag Waste Reduction Act & NYC 5-Cent Paper Bag Fee
agency: NYS Department of Environmental Conservation (DEC); NYC 5-cent paper bag fee collected by retailers
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于所有需征收纽约州销售税的零售商；5美分纸袋费适用于纽约市 / Applies to all NYS sales-tax-collecting retailers; the 5-cent paper bag fee applies in NYC.
keyRequirements: Retailers required to collect NYS sales tax may not provide single-use plastic carryout bags, and in NYC they must charge consumers a 5-cent fee for each paper carryout bag provided (with the fee separately reflected to the customer).
recommendedAction: 停止使用一次性塑料袋，配置纸袋并在 POS 中设置每个纸袋 5 美分收费 / Eliminate single-use plastic bags and configure the POS to charge the 5-cent fee per paper bag in NYC.
chineseSummary: 纽约州《减少购物袋废弃物法》自 2020 年 3 月 1 日起禁止需征收销售税的零售商提供一次性塑料购物袋；执法于 2020 年 10 月 19 日开始。纽约市已采纳每个纸质购物袋向消费者收取 5 美分的费用。商户须依规向顾客收取并在交易中体现该费用。
englishSummary: New York's Bag Waste Reduction Act (effective March 1, 2020; enforcement began Oct. 19, 2020) bars sales-tax-collecting retailers from distributing single-use plastic carryout bags. NYC has adopted the authorized 5-cent fee on each paper carryout bag, which retailers must collect from and reflect to the customer. DEC enforces the bag ban statewide.

regulationName: Posting of Calorie Information (NYC Health Code § 81.50)
chineseTitle: 纽约市连锁餐饮卡路里标示规定
englishTitle: NYC Calorie Labeling for Chain Food Establishments
agency: NYC Department of Health and Mental Hygiene (DOHMH)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于全美≥15家同名、菜品大致相同的连锁餐饮场所 / Applies to chains of 15+ locations nationally under the same name with substantially the same menu items.
keyRequirements: Food service establishments that are part of a chain with 15 or more locations nationally doing business under the same name and offering substantially the same menu items must post calorie counts for standard menu items on menus and menu boards.
complaintEnforcementRecord: Upheld by the Second Circuit in New York State Restaurant Ass'n v. NYC Board of Health, 556 F.3d 114 (2009), confirming DOHMH's authority to require calorie posting by covered chains.
recommendedAction: 在达到15家门店门槛时，于所有菜单板与菜单上加注标准菜品卡路里 / Add calorie counts for standard items to all menu boards and menus upon reaching the 15-location threshold.
chineseSummary: 纽约市卫生法典 §81.50 要求“覆盖场所”——即全国拥有 15 家及以上同名门店、提供大致相同菜品的连锁餐饮——在菜单和菜单板上标示各标准菜品的卡路里含量。该规定为全美首创（最初 2006 年提出，2008 年开始执法），现与 FDA 联邦菜单标示规则对齐。
englishSummary: NYC Health Code § 81.50 requires 'covered establishments' — chains with 15 or more locations nationwide under the same name offering substantially the same items — to post calorie counts for standard menu items on menus and menu boards. It was the first such law in the U.S. (proposed 2006, enforced from 2008) and now aligns with the FDA federal menu-labeling rule.

regulationName: Deceptive Acts and Practices & False Advertising (General Business Law §§ 349 & 350)
chineseTitle: 纽约州欺骗行为与虚假广告法（GBL §349、§350）
englishTitle: NY Deceptive Acts & False Advertising (GBL §§ 349 & 350)
agency: New York State Office of the Attorney General (OAG)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于在纽约州内开展业务的所有商家 / Applies to any business conducting business in New York State.
keyRequirements: Prohibits deceptive acts or practices and false/misleading advertising in the conduct of any business in New York; the Attorney General may seek injunctions, restitution, and civil penalties (up to $5,000 per violation), and consumers have a private right of action.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The NY AG routinely brings GBL §§ 349/350 actions against businesses for misleading pricing and advertising; it is among the most-litigated consumer statutes in New York, including frequent private 'deceptive pricing' class actions.
recommendedAction: 对所有广告、促销与应用内优惠进行真实性审查，确保条款充分披露、无误导 / Vet all advertising, promotions and in-app offers for accuracy and clear disclosure of conditions.
chineseSummary: 《一般商业法》第349条禁止在纽约州从事任何商业活动中的欺骗性行为或做法，第350条禁止虚假/误导性广告。总检察长可申请禁令、追缴及每项违规最高 5,000 美元的民事罚款，消费者亦享有私人诉权（可获实际损害赔偿，欺骗行为最高三倍损害但上限较低）。这是纽约州消费者保护的基础性法律。
englishSummary: GBL § 349 outlaws deceptive acts or practices, and § 350 outlaws false advertising, in the conduct of any business in New York. The Attorney General can seek injunctions, restitution and civil penalties of up to $5,000 per violation, and consumers have a private right of action for actual damages (with capped enhanced damages for willful conduct). These are New York's foundational consumer-protection statutes.

regulationName: FAIR Business Practices Act (Ch. 708 of 2025; S8416 — amends GBL § 349)
chineseTitle: 纽约州FAIR商业行为法（扩大对不公平/滥用行为的禁止）
englishTitle: NY FAIR Business Practices Act (Unfair/Abusive Practices Amendment)
agency: New York State Office of the Attorney General (OAG)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于在纽约州内开展业务的所有商家 / Applies to any business conducting business in New York State.
keyRequirements: Expands GBL § 349 for the first time in ~45 years to prohibit 'unfair' and 'abusive' acts or practices (in addition to 'deceptive'), eliminates the court-created 'consumer-oriented' limitation on AG enforcement, and broadens the Attorney General's enforcement reach.
recommendedAction: 复核促销、订阅与默认设置，确保不仅“非欺骗”而且不构成不公平或滥用（该法已于2026年2月17日生效）/ Review promotions, subscriptions and defaults so they are not merely non-deceptive but also not unfair or abusive (the Act took effect Feb. 17, 2026).
chineseSummary: 《培育可负担与诚信的合理商业行为法》（FAIR Act，2025年第708章，S8416）由霍楚州长于2025年12月19日签署，自2026年2月17日生效。该法约45年来首次实质性修订《一般商业法》第349条，将禁止范围从“欺骗”扩展到“不公平”和“滥用”行为，取消总检察长执法中“面向消费者”的限制，并扩大执法范围。其“不公平/滥用”定义借鉴联邦法。
englishSummary: The Fostering Affordability and Integrity through Reasonable (FAIR) Business Practices Act (Ch. 708 of 2025, S8416), signed Dec. 19, 2025 and effective Feb. 17, 2026, makes the first substantial update to GBL § 349 in roughly 45 years. It expands the statute from 'deceptive' to also reach 'unfair' and 'abusive' acts or practices, removes the 'consumer-oriented' limitation on AG actions, and broadens enforcement authority, drawing 'unfair/abusive' definitions from federal law.

regulationName: Consumer Protection Law — Unfair/Deceptive Trade Practices (NYC Admin. Code § 20-700)
chineseTitle: 纽约市消费者保护法——禁止不公平/欺骗性交易行为
englishTitle: NYC Consumer Protection Law — Unfair Trade Practices
agency: NYC Department of Consumer and Worker Protection (DCWP)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于在纽约市向消费者销售商品或服务的所有商家 / Applies to any business selling consumer goods or services in NYC.
keyRequirements: Prohibits any person from engaging in deceptive or unconscionable trade practices in the sale, lease, or offering of consumer goods or services, including false advertising, phony sales, hidden conditions/fees, and intentionally difficult cancellations.
complaintEnforcementRecord: DCWP regularly charges businesses under § 20-700 and its rules; under its 2026 'junk fee and subscription trap' initiative it has stepped up enforcement against hidden fees and hard-to-cancel offers.
recommendedAction: 确保门店与App的促销、定价和取消流程透明，无隐藏条件或费用 / Ensure in-store and in-app promotions, pricing and cancellation flows are transparent with no hidden conditions or fees.
chineseSummary: 纽约市行政法典 §20-700 禁止任何人在销售、租赁或提供消费品/服务时从事欺骗性或显失公平的交易行为，包括虚假广告、虚假打折、隐藏条件或费用、以及故意为难取消等。DCWP 依据该法及其实施细则（6 RCNY 第5章）执法，可处以罚款并要求退款。这是纽约市层面的核心消费者保护条款。
englishSummary: NYC Admin. Code § 20-700 bars deceptive or unconscionable trade practices in the sale, lease or offering of consumer goods or services — including false advertising, phony 'sales,' hidden conditions or fees, and intentionally difficult cancellations. DCWP enforces it (with implementing rules in 6 RCNY Ch. 5) through fines and restitution. It is NYC's core consumer-protection provision, separate from state law.

regulationName: New York Privacy Act (S3044, 2025-2026 Session) — PROPOSED
chineseTitle: 纽约州隐私法案（拟议，S3044）
englishTitle: New York Privacy Act (Proposed, S3044)
agency: New York State Legislature / (would be enforced by NYS Attorney General)
applicabilityThreshold: 拟议；将适用于处理纽约居民个人数据并达到规模门槛的企业 / Proposed; would apply to businesses processing NY residents' personal data above scope thresholds.
keyRequirements: Would require businesses that process New Yorkers' personal data to obtain opt-in consent before processing, provide rights to access/correct/delete data and to learn which third parties received it, and disclose de-identification methods.
recommendedAction: 监控立法进展；现在即对App与忠诚度计划的数据采集与同意流程进行盘点，为可能的opt-in要求做准备 / Monitor the bill; inventory app/loyalty data collection and consent flows now to prepare for a possible opt-in regime.
chineseSummary: 《纽约隐私法》（S3044，2025-2026会期）为拟议中的综合性消费者数据隐私立法，已于2025年5月通过参议院互联网与技术委员会，但尚未在两院通过，亦未送交州长签署。若通过，将要求处理纽约居民个人数据的企业在处理前取得选择加入（opt-in）同意，赋予消费者访问、更正、删除数据及获知数据共享对象的权利，并要求披露去标识化方法。
englishSummary: The New York Privacy Act (S3044, 2025-2026 session) is a proposed comprehensive consumer data-privacy bill that cleared the Senate Internet and Technology Committee in May 2025 but has not passed both chambers or reached the Governor and is not yet enacted. If passed, it would require businesses processing New Yorkers' personal data to obtain opt-in consent before processing and grant consumers rights to access, correct, and delete data and to learn which third parties received it, plus disclosure of de-identification methods.

regulationName: Executive Orders on Junk Fees & 'Subscription Tricks and Traps' (2026) directing DCWP enforcement
chineseTitle: 纽约市打击隐藏费用与订阅陷阱的行政命令（2026）
englishTitle: NYC Junk Fees & Subscription Traps Executive Orders (2026)
agency: Office of the Mayor of NYC / NYC Department of Consumer and Worker Protection (DCWP)
applicabilityThreshold: 政策方向性举措；具体新规将界定适用行业，餐饮/App订阅可能在内 / Policy-direction initiative; specific rules will define covered industries, potentially including food service/app subscriptions.
keyRequirements: Directs DCWP to escalate enforcement against hidden 'junk fees' and deceptive subscription/cancellation 'tricks and traps' under the Consumer Protection Law, and to consider new rules requiring all-in pricing and easy cancellation across consumer-facing industries.
complaintEnforcementRecord: Under the initiative, DCWP issued a hotel junk-fees rule (effective Feb. 22, 2026) and stepped up enforcement against hard-to-cancel gym, app, and subscription practices in early 2026.
recommendedAction: 主动审查所有面向消费者的费用与App订阅取消流程，确保全包定价与一键取消，以领先于可能的新规 / Proactively review consumer-facing fees and app subscription cancellation flows for all-in pricing and one-click cancellation ahead of likely new rules.
chineseSummary: 2026年1月5日，纽约市市长Mamdani签署两项行政命令（设立全市垃圾费用工作组并指示打击订阅陷阱），指示 DCWP 依据《消费者保护法》加强对隐藏“垃圾费用”和欺骗性订阅/取消“陷阱”的执法，并考虑出台要求全包定价（all-in pricing）和便捷取消的新规则。首批举措已包括针对酒店隐藏费用的规则（2026年2月22日生效）。这预示对所有面向消费者行业（含餐饮）更广泛的定价透明与订阅监管。
englishSummary: On Jan. 5, 2026, NYC's Mayor signed executive orders directing DCWP to escalate enforcement against hidden 'junk fees' and deceptive subscription/cancellation 'tricks and traps' under the Consumer Protection Law, and to weigh new all-in-pricing and easy-cancellation rules. The first concrete output is a hotel junk-fees rule (effective Feb. 22, 2026), signaling broader pricing-transparency and subscription scrutiny across consumer-facing industries, potentially including food service.

regulationName: Restaurant Letter Grading — Public Posting (NYC Health Code § 81.51; 24 RCNY Ch. 23)
chineseTitle: 纽约市餐饮卫生字母评级公开张贴规定
englishTitle: NYC Restaurant Letter Grade Public Posting
agency: NYC Department of Health and Mental Hygiene (DOHMH)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于纽约市内须接受字母评级的所有餐饮服务场所 / Applies to all NYC food service establishments subject to letter grading.
keyRequirements: Food service establishments must conspicuously post the DOHMH-issued letter grade (A/B/C) reflecting their sanitary inspection score where it can easily be seen by passersby.
complaintEnforcementRecord: DOHMH issues violations and fines to establishments that fail to post the required grade card; grade-posting compliance is a standard part of every sanitary inspection cycle.
recommendedAction: 确保每家门店在显著位置正确张贴最新字母评级卡，并维持卫生标准争取A级 / Ensure each store properly posts its current letter-grade card in a visible spot and maintains sanitation standards to earn an A.
chineseSummary: 自2010年7月起，纽约市卫生法典 §81.51 及 24 RCNY 第23章要求所有餐饮场所公开张贴卫生检查的字母评级（A 为0-13分、B 为14-27分、C 为28分及以上），张贴位置须显著、便于过往行人看到。DOHMH 至少每年进行一次检查并据此评级，未按规张贴会被罚款。
englishSummary: Since July 2010, NYC Health Code § 81.51 and 24 RCNY Ch. 23 require food service establishments to conspicuously post the DOHMH letter grade (A = 0-13 points, B = 14-27, C = 28+) from their sanitary inspection where passersby can easily see it. DOHMH inspects at least annually and assigns the grade; failure to properly post the grade card is itself finable.

regulationName: New Jersey Consumer Fraud Act (NJCFA), N.J.S.A. 56:8-1 et seq. (§56:8-2)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州消费者欺诈法（核心反欺诈条款）
englishTitle: New Jersey Consumer Fraud Act — Unlawful Practices
agency: NJ Division of Consumer Affairs / Office of the Attorney General (Office of Consumer Protection)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于所有在新泽西销售商品或服务的经营者，无规模门槛。/ Applies to any seller of merchandise or services in NJ; no size threshold.
keyRequirements: Prohibits any unconscionable commercial practice, deception, false promise, misrepresentation, or knowing omission of a material fact in connection with the sale or advertisement of any merchandise.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The NJ Attorney General and Division of Consumer Affairs regularly bring CFA enforcement actions and obtain civil penalties; CFA is the most-litigated consumer statute in NJ, frequently invoked in private class actions over pricing and advertising.
recommendedAction: 审查所有菜单、促销标识和广告，确保价格、折扣和产品描述真实无误。/ Audit all menus, promo signage, and ads so price, discount, and product claims are truthful and not misleading.
chineseSummary: 新泽西州《消费者欺诈法》是该州最重要的消费者保护基础法律，禁止任何在销售或广告商品过程中的欺诈、虚假承诺、不实陈述或故意隐瞒重大事实的行为。违法者可面临每次最高10,000美元（再犯20,000美元）的民事罚款，且消费者可起诉请求三倍赔偿及律师费。该法适用于咖啡店的菜单价格、促销、广告及收据等一切对外销售环节。
englishSummary: The NJCFA is New Jersey's foundational consumer-protection statute, barring deception, false promises, misrepresentation, and knowing omission of material facts in the sale or advertisement of merchandise. Violations carry civil penalties up to $10,000 first offense / $20,000 subsequent, and consumers can sue for treble (3x) damages plus attorneys' fees. It reaches a café's menu pricing, promotions, advertising, and receipts.

regulationName: Refund Policy Disclosure Act, N.J.S.A. 56:8-2.14 to 56:8-2.16
chineseTitle: 退款政策披露法（零售店须张贴退款政策）
englishTitle: Refund Policy Disclosure Act — Posting Requirement
agency: NJ Division of Consumer Affairs / Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于所有对退款设限的零售场所。/ Applies to any retail establishment that limits refunds.
keyRequirements: Every retail establishment that imposes any condition or limitation on refunds must conspicuously post a sign disclosing all material conditions of its refund policy; if no sign is posted, the consumer is entitled to a refund.
recommendedAction: 在每个新泽西门店收银台显著张贴退款/换货政策标识。/ Post a conspicuous refund/exchange-policy sign at the register in every NJ location.
chineseSummary: 该法要求每个对退款设有限制的零售场所必须显著张贴标识，披露其退款政策的所有重要条件（如"概不退款""仅可换货""限7天内"等）。如果未按规定张贴，消费者有权在20天内凭收据获得退款。违反该法构成《消费者欺诈法》项下的违法行为。
englishSummary: This Act requires every retail establishment that restricts refunds to conspicuously post a sign disclosing all material conditions of its refund policy (e.g., 'no refunds,' 'exchange only,' time limits). If no compliant sign is posted, the consumer may obtain a refund within 20 days with proof of purchase. A violation is an unlawful practice under the Consumer Fraud Act.

regulationName: General Advertising Rules, N.J.A.C. 13:45A-9.1 et seq. (esp. 9.2 and 9.6 fictitious pricing)
chineseTitle: 通用广告规则（禁止虚假定价与诱饵广告）
englishTitle: General Advertising & Fictitious Pricing Rules
agency: NJ Division of Consumer Affairs
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于所有在新泽西投放商品广告的卖方。/ Applies to any seller advertising merchandise in NJ.
keyRequirements: Advertisers must substantiate price/quality claims, maintain advertised stock to meet demand, clearly disclose limiting conditions and disclaimers, and may not use fictitious former prices or 'bait' advertising.
recommendedAction: 确保"原价""特价""限时"等促销用语真实有据，并备足广告商品库存。/ Ensure 'was/now,' 'sale,' and limited-time claims are truthful and substantiated, and stock advertised items adequately.
chineseSummary: 新泽西消费者事务司的通用广告规则要求广告主对其价格、质量等主张提供证据支持，备足广告商品库存以满足合理需求，并以醒目方式披露任何限制条件和免责声明。规则明确禁止使用虚构的"原价"进行比较定价（虚假定价），以及"诱饵广告"。违反者按《消费者欺诈法》论处。
englishSummary: The Division's general advertising rules require advertisers to substantiate price and quality claims, keep enough advertised stock for anticipated demand, and clearly and conspicuously disclose limiting conditions and disclaimers. The rules expressly prohibit fictitious former-price comparisons and bait advertising. Violations are treated as Consumer Fraud Act violations.

regulationName: Gift Card / Gift Certificate Law, N.J.S.A. 56:8-110
chineseTitle: 礼品卡与礼券法（有效期与休眠费限制）
englishTitle: Gift Card & Gift Certificate Rules
agency: NJ Division of Consumer Affairs / Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于在新泽西发行或销售礼品卡/礼券的商家。/ Applies to any merchant issuing or selling gift cards/certificates in NJ.
keyRequirements: Gift cards/certificates may not expire within 24 months of sale, no dormancy fee may be charged within 24 months (and any later fee is capped), terms must be disclosed in at least 10-point font, and a sub-$5 remaining balance must be refunded in cash on request.
recommendedAction: 确保新泽西发行的礼品卡无24个月内过期/休眠费，披露字体≥10号，并准备现金退还低于5美元的余额。/ Ensure NJ gift cards have no <24-month expiry/dormancy fee, disclose terms in ≥10-pt font, and honor cash-back on sub-$5 balances.
chineseSummary: 该法规定礼品卡/礼券自销售之日起24个月内不得过期，且24个月内不得收取休眠费（之后的费用亦受限）。所有有效期或休眠费条款须以至少10号字体在卡、收据或包装上披露，并提供咨询电话。自2012年9月1日起，若卡内余额低于5美元，持卡人有权要求商家以现金退还余额。
englishSummary: Gift cards and certificates may not expire within 24 months of sale, and no dormancy fee may be charged within that window (any later fee is capped). Expiration and fee terms must be disclosed in at least 10-point font with an information phone number. Since Sept 1, 2012, when a redeemed card has under $5 remaining, the holder may demand a cash refund of the balance.

regulationName: Price Gouging During Declared Emergency, N.J.S.A. 56:8-107 to 56:8-109
chineseTitle: 紧急状态期间禁止哄抬物价法
englishTitle: Price Gouging Law (Declared State of Emergency)
agency: NJ Division of Consumer Affairs / Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: 仅在州长宣布紧急状态期间及其后30天内适用。/ Applies only during a Governor-declared emergency and for 30 days after.
keyRequirements: During a declared state of emergency and for 30 days after, it is an unlawful practice to sell goods or services at a price more than 10% higher than the pre-emergency price (absent a corresponding cost increase).
complaintEnforcementRecord: The NJ Attorney General has repeatedly issued price-gouging warnings and opened investigations during declared emergencies (e.g., winter storms and the COVID-19 emergency), with thousands of complaints filed and subpoenas/penalties pursued.
recommendedAction: 在新泽西宣布紧急状态期间冻结价格，避免任何超过10%的涨价。/ Freeze menu prices during any NJ declared emergency; avoid any increase exceeding 10%.
chineseSummary: 在新泽西州宣布进入紧急状态期间及结束后30天内，以高于紧急状态前正常售价超过10%的价格销售商品或服务（且非因成本相应上升）构成《消费者欺诈法》项下的非法哄抬物价行为。违反者面临每次最高10,000美元、再犯20,000美元的民事罚款。该法在风暴、疫情等紧急状态下被积极执法。
englishSummary: During a declared state of emergency and for 30 days afterward, selling any merchandise or service for more than 10% above its pre-emergency price (without a matching cost increase) is unlawful price gouging under the Consumer Fraud Act. Penalties run to $10,000 first offense / $20,000 subsequent. The AG actively enforces this around storms and public-health emergencies.

regulationName: Data Breach Notification (Consumer Fraud Act amendment), N.J.S.A. 56:8-161 to 56:8-166; P.L. 2019, c.95
chineseTitle: 数据泄露通知法（2019年扩大个人信息定义）
englishTitle: Data Breach Notification Law (2019 amendment)
agency: NJ Division of State Police / Division of Consumer Affairs / Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于任何在NJ经营并保有NJ居民个人信息的企业。/ Applies to any business in NJ that maintains NJ residents' personal information.
keyRequirements: Any business that maintains computerized records must notify affected NJ residents (and the State Police) without unreasonable delay following discovery of a breach of personal information, which since 2019 includes online account credentials (username/email plus password or security question).
recommendedAction: 建立泄露响应流程：发现涉及NJ居民的泄露时及时通知用户并向州警报告。/ Maintain a breach-response plan to promptly notify affected NJ customers and report to State Police.
chineseSummary: 任何在新泽西经营并保有计算机化个人信息记录的企业，在发现数据泄露后须尽快（不得无理拖延）通知受影响的新泽西居民，并向州警备案。2019年9月1日生效的修订将"个人信息"定义扩展至在线账户凭证（用户名/邮箱加密码或安全问题答案）。该法纳入《消费者欺诈法》执法体系。
englishSummary: Any business holding computerized personal-information records must notify affected NJ residents (and the State Police) without unreasonable delay after discovering a breach. The Sept 1, 2019 amendment expanded 'personal information' to include online-account credentials (username/email plus password or security question/answer). Enforcement runs through the Consumer Fraud Act framework.

regulationName: Automatic Renewal / Service-Contract Cancellation Law, N.J.S.A. 56:12-95.1 et seq.; P.L. 2022, c.91
chineseTitle: 自动续约/服务合同取消法（2023年生效）
englishTitle: Automatic Renewal & Cancellation Law (2022 Act)
agency: NJ Division of Consumer Affairs / Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: 主要针对自动续约的服务合同；普通咖啡零售一般不涉及，除非推出订阅/会员自动续费。/ Aimed at auto-renewing service contracts; ordinary coffee retail is outside scope unless a subscription/membership auto-renews.
keyRequirements: Covered service contracts that auto-renew must offer an easy cancellation method (online, mail, or phone), acknowledge cancellation requests within five business days, include cancellation info in renewal notices, and give 30–60 days' notice before changing cancellation methods.
recommendedAction: 若推出自动续费会员/订阅，确保提供简便取消渠道并在5个工作日内确认请求。/ If launching auto-renewing memberships, provide easy cancellation and acknowledge requests within 5 business days.
chineseSummary: 该法（2022年颁布，2023年8月1日生效）针对自动续约的服务合同，要求经营者提供便捷的取消方式（在线、邮寄或电话），在收到取消请求后5个工作日内予以确认，在续约提醒中包含取消方式信息，并在变更取消方式前提前30–60天通知。不合规将允许消费者取消并获得按比例退款。
englishSummary: This 2022 Act (effective Aug 1, 2023) governs auto-renewing service contracts, requiring an easy cancellation method (online, mail, or phone), acknowledgment of cancellation requests within five business days, cancellation information in renewal reminders, and 30–60 days' notice before changing cancellation methods. Non-compliance lets the subscriber cancel and obtain a prorated refund.

regulationName: Credit Card Surcharge Disclosure & Cost Cap, N.J.S.A. 56:8-156.1 and 56:8-156.2; P.L. 2023, c.146
chineseTitle: 信用卡附加费披露与成本上限法（2023年）
englishTitle: Credit Card Surcharge Disclosure & Cost-Cap Law
agency: NJ Division of Consumer Affairs / Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: 仅在商家对信用卡付款收取附加费时适用（附加费非强制）。/ Applies only if the seller chooses to surcharge credit-card payments (surcharging is optional).
keyRequirements: A seller imposing a credit-card surcharge must disclose the surcharge amount before the consumer incurs a charge — for non-restaurants on signs at the point of entry and point of sale; for restaurants on signs in the service area and on the menu (including online/QR menus) — and the surcharge may not exceed the seller's actual cost to process the card.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The Division of Consumer Affairs investigates surcharge complaints under the Consumer Fraud Act; its 2023 consumer brief warns that undisclosed or excessive surcharges expose sellers to penalties of up to $10,000 (first) / $20,000 (subsequent).
recommendedAction: 若收取刷卡附加费，须在服务区和菜单（含线上/二维码菜单）披露金额，且金额不超过实际处理成本。/ If surcharging cards, disclose the exact amount in the service area and on all menus (incl. online/QR), capped at actual processing cost.
chineseSummary: 新泽西2023年颁布的法律（P.L. 2023, c.146）规定，若商家对信用卡付款收取附加费，必须在消费者产生费用前披露具体附加费金额，且附加费不得超过商家处理该卡的实际成本。对餐饮场所，须在顾客服务区及菜单（含线上/二维码菜单）上披露；对其他商家，须在入口及收银处张贴标识。违反者按《消费者欺诈法》论处，首次罚款最高10,000美元。
englishSummary: Under P.L. 2023, c.146, a seller that charges a credit-card surcharge must disclose the surcharge amount before the consumer incurs any charge and may not charge more than its actual card-processing cost. Restaurants must post the surcharge in the customer service area and on the menu (including online/QR menus); other sellers must post at the point of entry and point of sale. Violations are enforced under the Consumer Fraud Act ($10,000 first offense).

regulationName: New Jersey Data Privacy Act (NJDPA), N.J.S.A. 56:8-166.4 et seq.; S332 / P.L. 2023, c.266
chineseTitle: 新泽西数据隐私法（2025年1月生效）
englishTitle: New Jersey Data Privacy Act (NJDPA)
agency: NJ Division of Consumer Affairs / Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: 控制/处理≥10万NJ消费者数据，或≥2.5万NJ消费者且从数据销售获利的企业。/ Applies to controllers handling data of ≥100,000 NJ consumers, or ≥25,000 NJ consumers while deriving revenue from data sales.
keyRequirements: Controllers meeting the data thresholds must honor consumer rights to access, correct, delete, and port personal data and to opt out of sale/targeted advertising (including via universal opt-out signals like Global Privacy Control), processing opt-outs within 15 days.
recommendedAction: 评估是否达到门槛（≥10万NJ消费者，或≥2.5万消费者且销售数据获利），并部署隐私政策、退出机制与GPC识别。/ Assess if thresholds are met (≥100k NJ consumers, or ≥25k plus data-sale revenue) and deploy a privacy policy, opt-out, and GPC recognition.
chineseSummary: 新泽西数据隐私法于2025年1月15日生效，赋予新泽西居民访问、更正、删除、转移个人数据以及选择退出数据出售和定向广告的权利。达到门槛的"控制者"必须在15天内处理退出请求，并识别通用退出机制（如Global Privacy Control）。法律设有违规通知整改期（至2026年7月1日为30天整改窗口）。由州总检察长独家执法，无私人诉权。
englishSummary: Effective Jan 15, 2025, the NJDPA gives NJ residents rights to access, correct, delete, and port their personal data and to opt out of sale and targeted advertising, including via universal opt-out signals such as Global Privacy Control. Covered controllers must process opt-outs within 15 days, and (until July 1, 2026) get a 30-day cure period after notice. The AG has exclusive enforcement; there is no private right of action.

regulationName: Consumer Subscription Cancellation Standards Act (S3877 / A5395), 2024–2025 Session
chineseTitle: 消费者订阅取消标准法案（拟议，2026年初被附条件否决）
englishTitle: Consumer Subscription Cancellation Standards Act (pending)
agency: NJ Legislature (would be enforced by NJ Division of Consumer Affairs / AG)
applicabilityThreshold: 如成法，将适用于向NJ消费者提供自动续约订阅/会员的经营者。/ If enacted, would apply to businesses offering auto-renewing subscriptions/memberships to NJ consumers.
keyRequirements: Would broadly require any subscription/negative-option service to provide a simple ('click-to-cancel') online cancellation method, clear disclosure of auto-renewal terms, and renewal/price-change reminders for consumers.
recommendedAction: 监测该法案进展；若日后成法，确保任何NJ订阅/会员产品提供一键在线取消。/ Monitor this bill; if enacted, ensure any NJ subscription/membership offers one-click online cancellation.
chineseSummary: S3877（与A5395合并）拟为所有订阅服务和"负面选项"功能设立取消标准，要求提供简便的在线取消（"一键取消"）方式、清晰披露自动续约条款，并发送续约及价格变更提醒。该法案于2025年12月经参众两院通过，但于2026年1月12日被州长附条件否决，目前尚未成为法律，须待立法机关回应否决意见。
englishSummary: S3877 (joined with A5395) would set cancellation standards for all subscription services and negative-option features, requiring an easy online ('click-to-cancel') cancellation method, clear disclosure of auto-renewal terms, and renewal/price-change reminders. Both chambers passed it in December 2025, but it received a conditional veto on Jan 12, 2026 and is not yet law pending legislative response.

regulationName: California Civil Code Section 1723 — Retail Seller Refund Policy Posting
chineseTitle: 加州退款政策张贴要求（民法典第1723条）
englishTitle: California Refund Policy Posting Requirement (Civ. Code § 1723)
agency: California Department of Justice / Office of the Attorney General (private enforcement)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to retail sellers of non-perishable goods to the public; retail merchandise (mugs, beans) — not the food/beverage transaction itself. / 适用于向公众销售非易腐商品的零售商。
keyRequirements: Any retail seller whose policy is not to give a full cash/credit refund or equal exchange for at least seven days after purchase (with proof of purchase) must conspicuously post that policy at each cash register, sales counter, or public entrance, or on item tags/order forms.
recommendedAction: Post a compliant refund-policy sign at every register/counter for retail merchandise sales; exempt perishable food items / 对零售商品在每个收银台张贴合规退款政策标识。
chineseSummary: 加州零售商若不提供购买后至少7天内的全额现金退款、店内credit或等值换货，必须在收银台、销售柜台或公共入口处显著张贴其退款政策，注明退款形式、期限、适用商品及条件。若未按要求张贴，顾客可凭购买凭证在30天内退货并要求全额退款。食品等易腐商品、定制商品和已使用商品可豁免。
englishSummary: A California retail seller that does not offer a full cash or credit refund or equal exchange for at least seven days after purchase must conspicuously post its policy (form of refund, time period, covered goods, conditions) at each register, counter, or entrance, or on tags/order forms. If the policy is not posted, the buyer may return the goods with proof of purchase within 30 days for a full refund. Perishables, 'as-is,' custom, and used/damaged goods are exempt.

regulationName: California SB 478 — Honest Pricing Law / Hidden Fees Ban (Civ. Code § 1770(a)(29))
chineseTitle: 加州诚实定价法 / 隐藏费用禁令（SB 478）
englishTitle: California Honest Pricing Law — Hidden/Junk Fees Ban (SB 478)
agency: California Department of Justice / Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to nearly all businesses offering goods/services to California consumers; food/beverage mandatory fees have a conditional exemption via SB 1524. / 几乎适用于所有面向加州消费者的企业。
keyRequirements: Prohibits advertising or listing a price that does not include all mandatory fees and charges ('drip pricing'); the advertised price must be the full price the consumer pays, excluding only government taxes and reasonable shipping.
recommendedAction: Audit all menus, app, kiosk and ad prices to ensure displayed price includes every mandatory fee; if a food/bev surcharge is used, satisfy the SB 1524 disclosure rule. / 审核所有菜单和广告价格确保含全部强制费用。
chineseSummary: SB 478修订了《消费者法律救济法》（民法典第1770(a)(29)条），自2024年7月1日起禁止'滴漏式定价'——即广告价格未包含所有强制性费用。向消费者展示的价格必须是其实际需支付的全额，仅可另计政府税费和合理运费。违反者构成不公平商业行为，可被起诉。
englishSummary: Effective July 1, 2024, SB 478 amended the Consumers Legal Remedies Act (Civ. Code § 1770(a)(29)) to ban 'drip pricing' — advertising a price that omits mandatory fees. The price shown to consumers must be the full amount they pay, excluding only government taxes and reasonable shipping. Violations are actionable as unfair business practices.

regulationName: California SB 1524 — Restaurant/Food Mandatory-Fee Disclosure Exemption
chineseTitle: 加州餐饮强制费用披露豁免（SB 1524）
englishTitle: California Restaurant Mandatory-Fee Disclosure (SB 1524)
agency: California Department of Justice / Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to restaurants, bars, food concessions, grocery stores and grocery delivery operating in California. / 适用于在加州经营的餐厅、酒吧、食品摊位和杂货商。
keyRequirements: Exempts restaurants, bars, food concessions and grocery sellers from SB 478's all-in pricing rule only if any mandatory fee or charge is clearly and conspicuously displayed, with an explanation of its purpose, on any menu, advertisement, or other display showing the food/beverage price.
recommendedAction: If using any mandatory surcharge, add a conspicuous menu/ad disclosure stating the fee amount and purpose; otherwise fold it into the listed price. / 若收取强制附加费，须在菜单显著披露金额与用途。
chineseSummary: SB 1524为餐厅、酒吧、食品摊位和杂货商提供SB 478诚实定价法的豁免，但条件是：任何强制性费用或附加费必须在显示食品/饮料价格的菜单、广告或其他展示处，清晰显著地披露并说明其用途。自2024年7月1日起生效（紧急法案，2024年6月29日签署即时生效）。2025年7月1日起'清晰显著'须符合更严格的字体/对比技术标准。
englishSummary: SB 1524 carves restaurants, bars, food concessions and grocers out of SB 478's all-in pricing rule, but only if any mandatory fee/surcharge is clearly and conspicuously displayed with an explanation of its purpose on any menu, advertisement, or display showing the item price. Operative July 1, 2024 (signed June 29, 2024 as an urgency statute); from July 1, 2025 the 'clear and conspicuous' standard tightened to specific font/contrast requirements (per Civ. Code § 1791).

regulationName: California Unfair Competition Law & False Advertising Law (Bus. & Prof. Code §§ 17200, 17500)
chineseTitle: 加州不正当竞争法与虚假广告法（§17200/§17500）
englishTitle: California Unfair Competition Law / False Advertising Law (§§ 17200, 17500)
agency: California Attorney General / district attorneys (and private plaintiffs)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to all businesses operating in or directing commerce to California. / 适用于在加州经营或面向加州消费者的所有企业。
keyRequirements: Prohibits any unlawful, unfair, or fraudulent business act or practice and any untrue or misleading advertising; remedies include injunctions, restitution, and civil penalties up to $2,500 per violation (Bus. & Prof. Code § 17206).
recommendedAction: Substantiate all advertising/menu claims (e.g., 'fresh,' 'organic,' loyalty/promo terms) and keep pricing transparent; treat any other statutory violation as also UCL-actionable. / 确保所有广告与菜单宣称有据可查。
chineseSummary: 加州《不正当竞争法》（商业与职业法典第17200条）禁止任何非法、不公平或欺诈性的商业行为，以及不实或误导性广告；《虚假广告法》（第17500条）禁止任何与商品/服务销售相关的不实或误导性陈述。检察官和私人原告均可起诉，救济包括禁令、退赔和每项违规最高2500美元的民事罚款（第17206条）。这是加州消费者保护的基础性法律。
englishSummary: California's UCL (§ 17200) bars any unlawful, unfair, or fraudulent business act or practice, and the FAL (§ 17500) bars untrue or misleading advertising of goods/services. Both are enforceable by the AG, district attorneys, and private plaintiffs, with injunctions, restitution, and civil penalties up to $2,500 per violation (§ 17206). This is California's foundational consumer-protection backstop (state-law analog to FTC Act § 5).

regulationName: California Business & Professions Code § 12024.2 — Weights & Measures Price Accuracy (Scanner/Overcharge)
chineseTitle: 加州计量与价格准确性法（扫描价/多收费，§12024.2）
englishTitle: California Price Accuracy / Scanner Overcharge Law (B&P § 12024.2)
agency: County Agricultural Commissioner / Sealer of Weights & Measures; California Department of Food & Agriculture, Division of Measurement Standards
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to any seller charging for commodities at point of sale in California. / 适用于在加州销售点收费的任何商家。
keyRequirements: Makes it unlawful to charge a price greater than the lowest price advertised, posted, marked, displayed, or that scans for a commodity at the time of sale.
recommendedAction: Reconcile POS/menu-board prices with posted prices after every price change and resolve scanner overcharges immediately. / 每次调价后核对收银系统与张贴价格一致。
chineseSummary: 加州商业与职业法典第12024.2条规定，销售时向顾客收取高于该商品所标示、张贴、显示或扫描得出的最低价格属违法。多收超过1美元或属故意/重大过失者为轻罪，可处25–1000美元罚款；多收1美元及以下为违例，可处最高100美元罚款。县计量检验员会对收银扫描系统进行准确性抽查，确保扫描价与货架/标示价一致。
englishSummary: Under B&P § 12024.2 it is unlawful, at the time of sale, to charge more than the lowest price advertised, posted, marked, displayed, or scanned for a commodity. Overcharges over $1 (or willful/grossly negligent ones) are misdemeanors punishable by $25–$1,000 fines; overcharges of $1 or less are infractions (up to $100). County weights-and-measures sealers conduct scanner-accuracy inspections to ensure POS prices match shelf/posted prices.

regulationName: California Song-Beverly Credit Card Act — Surcharge Prohibition (Civ. Code § 1748.1)
chineseTitle: 加州Song-Beverly信用卡法 — 附加费禁令（§1748.1）
englishTitle: California Song-Beverly Credit Card Surcharge Prohibition (Civ. Code § 1748.1)
agency: California Department of Justice / Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to retailers in consumer sales, service, or lease transactions in California. / 适用于加州消费者销售、服务或租赁交易中的零售商。
keyRequirements: No retailer may impose a surcharge on a customer who elects to pay by credit card instead of cash/check, though a posted cash discount offered to all buyers is permitted.
recommendedAction: Do not add a credit-card surcharge; if differentiating, present it as a posted cash discount available to all customers. / 不要加收信用卡附加费，如需差价应以现金折扣形式张贴。
chineseSummary: 加州民法典第1748.1条（Song-Beverly信用卡法的一部分，1985年制定）禁止零售商对选择使用信用卡而非现金/支票付款的顾客加收附加费，但允许对所有顾客提供现金折扣。故意违规并在书面要求后30天内不退还的，须向持卡人赔偿三倍实际损失加律师费。注：2018年Italian Colors诉Becerra联邦判决限制了该法对'附加费'标示的执行，州总检察长仅对与该案情形相似的商家适用该判决。
englishSummary: Civ. Code § 1748.1 (part of the Song-Beverly Credit Card Act, enacted 1985) bars retailers from surcharging customers who pay by credit card rather than cash/check, though an across-the-board posted cash discount is allowed. Willful violations not cured within 30 days of written demand expose the retailer to treble actual damages plus attorney's fees. Note: the 2018 Italian Colors v. Becerra federal ruling limited enforcement as to how surcharges may be labeled; the AG applies that decision only to similarly situated merchants.

regulationName: California Gift Card / Gift Certificate Law (Civ. Code § 1749.5)
chineseTitle: 加州礼品卡法（民法典第1749.5条）
englishTitle: California Gift Card Law (Civ. Code § 1749.5)
agency: California Department of Justice / Office of the Attorney General (and private enforcement)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to sellers/issuers of gift certificates and gift cards usable in California. / 适用于在加州可使用的礼品卡的销售方/发行方。
keyRequirements: Gift certificates/cards sold for value generally may not contain an expiration date or service fees, and a card with a remaining balance below the statutory threshold must be redeemed in cash on request.
recommendedAction: Configure gift-card program with no expiration/fees and a POS workflow to cash out balances below the threshold on request. / 礼品卡不设有效期/费用，并支持小额余额现金兑付。
chineseSummary: 加州民法典第1749.5条规定，有价出售的礼品卡/礼券一般不得设有效期或服务费（少数例外），且无有效期者有效至兑换或更换为止。剩余余额低于法定门槛的礼品卡须应顾客要求兑换为现金。注：SB 22已将现金兑换门槛从10美元提高到15美元，自2026年4月1日起生效（见单独条目）。
englishSummary: Under Civ. Code § 1749.5, gift cards/certificates sold for value generally cannot carry an expiration date or service fees (limited exceptions), and one sold without an expiration date is valid until redeemed or replaced. A card with a remaining balance below the statutory threshold must be redeemed in cash on the holder's request. (SB 22 raises that cash-out threshold from $10 to under $15 effective April 1, 2026 — see separate entry.)

regulationName: California SB 22 — Gift Card Cash-Out Threshold Increased to $15 (amends Civ. Code § 1749.5)
chineseTitle: 加州SB 22 — 礼品卡现金兑换门槛提高至15美元
englishTitle: California SB 22 — Gift Card Cash-Out Threshold Raised to $15
agency: California Department of Justice / Office of the Attorney General (and private enforcement)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to sellers/issuers of gift cards usable in California; promotional cards excepted. / 适用于在加州可使用的礼品卡发行方；促销卡除外。
keyRequirements: Amends Civ. Code § 1749.5 so that a gift certificate/card with a remaining cash value of less than $15 (up from $10) must be redeemed in cash for its cash value on the holder's request.
recommendedAction: Reconfigure gift-card cash-out logic to the $15 threshold and train cashiers on the rule now in effect (since April 1, 2026). / 将现金兑付阈值更新为15美元并培训员工（自2026年4月1日起生效）。
chineseSummary: SB 22由州长Newsom于2025年10月1日签署（Stats. 2025, Ch. 207），修订民法典第1749.5条，将礼品卡强制现金兑换门槛从10美元提高到低于15美元，自2026年4月1日起生效。即剩余余额低于15美元的礼品卡须应顾客要求兑换为现金。这是该门槛自2008年以来的首次调整，原告律师预计将在新门槛生效后迅速测试合规性。
englishSummary: SB 22 (signed October 1, 2025, Stats. 2025, Ch. 207; operative April 1, 2026) amends Civ. Code § 1749.5 to raise the mandatory cash-out threshold from $10 to under $15. A gift card with a remaining balance under $15 must be redeemed in cash on the holder's request. It is the first change to the threshold since 2008, and the plaintiffs' bar is expected to test compliance quickly after the effective date.

regulationName: California Automatic Renewal Law as amended by AB 2863 (Bus. & Prof. Code §§ 17600–17606)
englishTitle: California Automatic Renewal Law (amended by AB 2863)
agency: California Department of Justice / Office of the Attorney General; district attorneys
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to businesses that make automatic-renewal or continuous-service offers to California consumers. / 适用于向加州消费者提供自动续约或持续服务的企业。
keyRequirements: Requires clear-and-conspicuous pre-purchase disclosure of automatic-renewal terms, separate affirmative consent, annual reminders, notice of material price/term changes, and an easy online 'click-to-cancel' method (and voice equivalent).
recommendedAction: If any auto-renewing membership/subscription exists, audit sign-up consent flow, add annual reminders and a one-click online cancel path. / 如有自动续费会员，须审核同意流程并提供一键取消。
chineseSummary: 加州自动续约法（商业与职业法典第17600–17606条）经AB 2863修订后，自2025年7月1日起对自动续约和持续服务（含免费试用转付费）施加更严格要求：购买前须清晰显著披露续约条款、单独获得明确同意、提供年度提醒、对重大价格/条款变更发出通知，并提供与注册方式同样便捷的在线'一键取消'（及语音等效）机制。新要求适用于2025年7月1日当日或之后签订、修订或延展的合同。适用于任何按周期向消费者收费的企业。
englishSummary: California's ARL (B&P §§ 17600–17606), as amended by AB 2863, took effect July 1, 2025 and tightens rules for any business that charges consumers on a recurring basis (including free-to-pay trial conversions): clear-and-conspicuous pre-purchase disclosure, separate affirmative consent, annual reminders, notice of material price/term changes, and an easy online 'click-to-cancel' method plus a voice equivalent. The amended requirements apply to contracts entered into, amended, or extended on or after July 1, 2025.

regulationName: California Proposition 65 — Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act (Health & Safety Code § 25249.6) with OEHHA Coffee Exemption (27 CCR § 25704)
chineseTitle: 加州65号提案（安全饮用水与毒性物质执法法）及咖啡豁免规定
englishTitle: California Proposition 65 Warning Law + OEHHA Coffee Exemption
agency: California Office of Environmental Health Hazard Assessment (OEHHA); enforced by the AG, district attorneys, and private plaintiffs
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to businesses with 10+ employees that expose Californians to listed chemicals; coffee-inherent chemicals are exempted. / 适用于雇员10人以上、使加州人接触列名化学品的企业；咖啡固有化学品已豁免。
keyRequirements: Businesses must give a clear and reasonable warning before knowingly exposing individuals to listed chemicals, but OEHHA regulation 27 CCR § 25704 establishes that chemicals (e.g., acrylamide) created by and inherent in roasting/brewing coffee pose no significant risk, exempting coffee from the warning requirement.
complaintEnforcementRecord: Council for Education and Research on Toxics (CERT) v. Starbucks et al. — Los Angeles Superior Court litigation that forced coffee acrylamide warnings until OEHHA adopted the § 25704 coffee exemption.
recommendedAction: Rely on the OEHHA coffee exemption for coffee, but review other products/materials for any required Prop 65 warnings. / 咖啡可依豁免，但需审查其他产品是否需Prop 65警告。
chineseSummary: 65号提案（《1986年安全饮用水与毒性物质执法法》，健康与安全法典第25249.6条）要求企业在使个人接触州列名致癌/致畸化学品前提供'清晰合理的警告'。咖啡因烘焙含丙烯酰胺曾引发诉讼（CERT诉星巴克等案）。OEHHA于2019年通过法规第25704条（2019年6月3日经OAL批准，2019年10月1日生效）认定咖啡烘焙/冲泡过程中固有产生的列名化学品不构成显著风险，使咖啡本身豁免警告。但店内其他产品/材料仍可能触发Prop 65警告义务。
englishSummary: Prop 65 (Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act of 1986; Health & Safety Code § 25249.6) requires a clear and reasonable warning before knowingly exposing people to listed chemicals. Coffee's acrylamide triggered major litigation (CERT v. Starbucks et al.), but OEHHA's regulation 27 CCR § 25704 (approved by OAL June 3, 2019; effective October 1, 2019) declares that listed chemicals inherent in roasting/brewing coffee pose no significant risk — exempting coffee itself from a warning. Other in-store products/exposures can still trigger Prop 65.

regulationName: CCPA Cybersecurity Audit, Risk Assessment & Automated Decisionmaking Technology (ADMT) Regulations (Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.100 et seq.)
chineseTitle: CCPA网络安全审计、风险评估与自动化决策技术（ADMT）法规
englishTitle: CCPA Cybersecurity Audit / Risk Assessment / ADMT Regulations
agency: California Privacy Protection Agency (CPPA)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to businesses meeting CCPA thresholds: >$26.625M annual gross revenue, or buying/selling/sharing personal info of 100,000+ consumers/households, or 50%+ revenue from selling/sharing personal info. / 适用于达到CCPA门槛的企业。
keyRequirements: Covered businesses must conduct documented privacy risk assessments and annual independent cybersecurity audits, and provide notice and consumer opt-out/access rights for significant decisions made using automated decisionmaking technology, with phased compliance deadlines through 2027–2030.
recommendedAction: Track whether CA operations cross CCPA thresholds as the chain grows; if so, schedule risk assessments and a cybersecurity-audit program ahead of the phased deadlines. / 监测是否达到CCPA门槛，提前规划风险评估与网络安全审计。
chineseSummary: 加州隐私保护局（CPPA）的新CCPA法规已于2025年9月22日获行政法办公室（OAL）批准并向州务卿备案，自2026年1月1日起生效，但部分要求设有分阶段合规期限。受监管企业须就高风险数据处理进行书面隐私风险评估、每年进行独立网络安全审计，并对使用自动化决策技术（ADMT）作出的重大决定提供通知及消费者退出/访问权。ADMT合规期限至2027年1月1日，网络安全审计义务按营收分阶段至2028–2030年。仅适用于达到CCPA门槛的企业。
englishSummary: The CPPA's new CCPA regulations (approved by OAL Sept. 22, 2025 and filed with the Secretary of State) take effect January 1, 2026 with phased compliance deadlines. Covered businesses must perform documented privacy risk assessments, undergo annual independent cybersecurity audits, and give notice plus consumer opt-out/access rights for significant decisions made with automated decisionmaking technology (ADMT). ADMT obligations phase in by Jan. 1, 2027 and cybersecurity-audit duties phase in by revenue tier through 2028–2030. Only businesses meeting CCPA thresholds are covered.

regulationName: Massachusetts Consumer Protection Act, M.G.L. c. 93A, § 2 (Unfair and Deceptive Acts and Practices)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州消费者保护法（93A章第2条）——禁止不公平和欺骗性行为
englishTitle: MA Consumer Protection Act (Chapter 93A) — UDAP Prohibition
agency: Massachusetts Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: Any person engaged in trade or commerce in Massachusetts. 在马萨诸塞州从事贸易或商业的任何主体。
keyRequirements: Prohibits unfair methods of competition and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any trade or commerce, interpreted consistently with FTC Act Section 5.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The AG routinely brings 93A enforcement; e.g., the 2025 'junk fee' regulations (940 CMR 38.00) were promulgated under 93A's rulemaking authority to curb deceptive fee practices.
recommendedAction: Treat 93A compliance as foundational; map every consumer-facing practice (pricing, refunds, ads, fees) to the relevant 940 CMR sub-regulation. 将93A合规作为基础，确保所有面向消费者的做法符合相应940 CMR细则。
chineseSummary: 93A章是马萨诸塞州的核心消费者保护法（1967年颁布，俗称"小FTC法"），宣布在任何贸易或商业活动中的不公平或欺骗性行为为非法。法院在解释该条时参照联邦贸易委员会及联邦法院对FTC法第5条的解释，且总检察长有权制定具体的实施细则（即940 CMR系列法规）。
englishSummary: Chapter 93A, enacted in 1967 as Massachusetts' "little FTC Act," declares unfair or deceptive acts or practices in any trade or commerce unlawful. Courts construe it in line with FTC interpretations of Section 5 of the FTC Act, and the Attorney General is authorized to promulgate implementing regulations (the 940 CMR series). Violations can trigger consumer suits with multiple/treble damages and attorney's fees.

regulationName: 940 CMR 3.13 — Pricing and Refund, Return and Cancellation Privileges
chineseTitle: 940 CMR 3.13——定价及退款、退货和取消政策的披露
englishTitle: 940 CMR 3.13 — Refund/Return Policy & Price Disclosure
agency: Massachusetts Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: Sellers in consumer transactions in Massachusetts. 在马萨诸塞州进行消费者交易的卖方。
keyRequirements: Sellers must clearly and conspicuously disclose the exact nature and extent of their refund, return, or cancellation policy to the buyer prior to the consummation of a transaction, and must mark or disclose prices before sale.
complaintEnforcementRecord: MA AG/consumer guidance specifically cautions that a return policy posted only on the receipt violates 940 CMR 3.13 because the consumer cannot see it before buying.
recommendedAction: Post a clear, conspicuous refund/return policy sign at each register/order point before purchase; do not rely on receipt-only disclosure. 在每个收银点购买前张贴清晰显著的退款/退货政策标识。
chineseSummary: 该法规规定，卖方未能在交易完成"之前"以清晰、显著的方式向买方披露其退款、退货或取消政策，以及未在售前标示价格，均构成93A章下的不公平或欺骗性行为。商家可以采用任何退款政策，但必须在购买前张贴公示；仅将政策印在收据上不符合要求，因为收据是在购买后才交付的。
englishSummary: This AG regulation makes it an unfair or deceptive act to fail to clearly and conspicuously disclose the seller's refund, return, or cancellation policy before a transaction is completed, and to fail to disclose price before sale. A store may adopt any policy it wishes but must post it conspicuously pre-purchase; printing it only on the receipt is insufficient because the receipt is given after the sale.

regulationName: 940 CMR 38.00 — Unfair and Deceptive Fees ("Junk Fee" & Negative Option Regulations)
chineseTitle: 940 CMR 38.00——不公平和欺骗性费用（"垃圾费用"及自动续订法规）
englishTitle: 940 CMR 38.00 — Junk Fee & Negative Option Rules
agency: Massachusetts Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: Businesses marketing/selling products for personal, family or household use in MA, including trial offers and negative-option contracts. 在马萨诸塞州为个人/家庭用途营销或销售产品的企业，包括试用和自动续订合同。
keyRequirements: Requires clear and conspicuous disclosure of the total price including all mandatory non-government fees before collecting consumer information, and mandates easy cancellation plus advance renewal reminders for trial offers and negative-option (auto-renewal) contracts.
complaintEnforcementRecord: AG Campbell's office issued the regulations and accompanying guidance documents in 2025 and made them enforceable by the AGO beginning September 2, 2025.
recommendedAction: Audit menu/app pricing to display total price with all mandatory fees up front; ensure any coffee subscription has one-click cancellation and pre-renewal reminders. 审查菜单/应用定价以预先显示含所有强制费用的总价，并为咖啡订阅提供便捷取消和续费提醒。
chineseSummary: 总检察长Campbell于2025年颁布的940 CMR 38.00法规自2025年9月2日起生效，要求企业在收集消费者信息前清晰、显著地披露包含所有强制性非政府费用的"总价"，并将总价显示得比其他价格信息更突出。对于试用优惠和带自动续订（negative option）特征的合同，法规要求提供至少与订购同样便捷的取消方式，以及在续费前5至30天内的书面提醒。
englishSummary: Promulgated by AG Campbell and effective September 2, 2025, 940 CMR 38.00 requires businesses to disclose the all-in total price (including every mandatory non-government fee) clearly and conspicuously before collecting consumer information, displayed more prominently than other pricing. For trial offers and negative-option (auto-renewal) contracts, it mandates cancellation at least as easy as sign-up and written renewal reminders 5–30 days before each recurring charge.

regulationName: 940 CMR 6.00 — Retail Advertising (General Requirements, incl. 940 CMR 6.04)
chineseTitle: 940 CMR 6.00——零售广告法规（含6.04一般要求）
englishTitle: 940 CMR 6.00 — Retail Advertising Disclosure Rules
agency: Massachusetts Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: Any advertisement by a retail seller in MA (other than motor-vehicle ads). 马萨诸塞州零售卖方的任何广告（机动车广告除外）。
keyRequirements: It is unfair or deceptive for a seller to fail to clearly and conspicuously disclose in any advertisement material facts (including mandatory add-on fees and price-comparison terms) whose omission would tend to mislead reasonable buyers.
recommendedAction: Review all promotional advertising so any mandatory fee and discount/comparison basis is disclosed clearly and conspicuously alongside the advertised price. 审查所有促销广告，确保强制费用和折扣比较依据与广告价格一同清晰显著披露。
chineseSummary: 940 CMR 6.00规范马萨诸塞州的零售广告。根据6.04的一般要求，卖方在任何广告中未能清晰、显著地披露可能误导合理买方的重要事实——例如除广告价格外还将收取的强制性附加费用（如配送、手续、安装费）以及价格比较和返利的条款——构成不公平或欺骗性行为。该法规是对940 CMR 3.00一般规定的补充。
englishSummary: 940 CMR 6.00 governs retail advertising in Massachusetts. Under the 6.04 general requirements, a seller commits an unfair or deceptive act by failing to clearly and conspicuously disclose material facts in any advertisement—such as mandatory add-on fees charged beyond the advertised price (delivery, handling, installation) and the terms of price-comparison or rebate claims—whose omission would tend to mislead reasonable buyers. The regulation supplements the 940 CMR 3.00 general rules.

regulationName: M.G.L. c. 94 §§ 184B–184E — Item Pricing Law, Price Accuracy & Scanner Accuracy
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州第94章184B-184E条——商品标价法、价格准确性与扫描准确性
englishTitle: MA Item Pricing & Scanner Accuracy Law (Ch. 94 §§184B–184E)
agency: Massachusetts Division of Standards (Office of Consumer Affairs) / local Sealers of Weights & Measures
applicabilityThreshold: Food stores and food departments selling packaged grocery items in MA (with a scanner-waiver program for stores using item-pricing alternatives). 在马萨诸塞州销售包装杂货的食品店及食品部门。
keyRequirements: Food/grocery stores must accurately price-mark or post prices and sell each item at the lowest advertised or marked price, meet a 98% scanner price-accuracy standard, and provide a price-accuracy guarantee (a free unit) when an item scans higher than its marked/advertised price.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The Division of Standards conducts annual scanner inspections; reported statewide pricing accuracy has exceeded 99.3%, above the 98% standard, with non-compliant stores subject to fines under §184E.
recommendedAction: Reconcile shelf tags, signage and POS pricing regularly; honor the lowest displayed price and the free-unit guarantee on scanner overcharges. 定期核对货架标签、标牌与POS定价，对扫描多收时执行最低价和免费赠品保证。
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州第94章184B-184E条要求食品/杂货商店准确标价或公示价格，并以商品、标牌或广告上标示的最低价格出售。商店每年须达到98%的扫描价格准确性标准（基于至少50件样品的随机抽样），并提供"价格准确性保证"——当某商品扫描价格高于其标示或广告价格时，立即免费赠送一件该商品。
englishSummary: M.G.L. c. 94 §§184B–184E require food/grocery stores to price-mark or post prices accurately and to sell each item at the lowest price marked, signed, or advertised. Stores must annually meet a 98% scanner price-accuracy standard (random sample of at least 50 items) and honor a price-accuracy guarantee—giving one free unit of any item that scans higher than its marked or advertised price. Local Sealers of Weights & Measures and the Division of Standards inspect and may fine for violations.

regulationName: M.G.L. c. 98 — Weights and Measures (Sealing & Annual Inspection of Commercial Devices)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州第98章——度量衡（商用计量器具的封印与年度检验）
englishTitle: MA Weights & Measures — Device Sealing & Inspection (Ch. 98)
agency: Massachusetts Division of Standards / local Sealers of Weights & Measures
applicabilityThreshold: Any business using commercial weighing/measuring devices for public sales in MA. 在马萨诸塞州使用商用计量器具进行公开销售的任何企业。
keyRequirements: All commercial weighing and measuring devices used in public sales (e.g., scales) must be tested, adjusted, and sealed annually by a municipal Sealer of Weights and Measures or state compliance officer before use in trade.
recommendedAction: Inventory any retail scales; schedule annual sealing by the local Sealer of Weights & Measures and keep sealing certificates on file. 盘点零售用秤，安排当地封印官的年度封印并存档封印证书。
chineseSummary: 根据马萨诸塞州第98章（及1998年第295号法案），用于公开销售的所有商用称重和计量器具——如商店的秤——必须由市政度量衡封印官或州合规官员每年进行测试、校准和封印。人口超过2万的市镇必须任命自己的封印官；州合规官负责约一半的市镇。
englishSummary: Under M.G.L. c. 98 (and c. 295 of the Acts of 1998), all commercial weighing and measuring devices used in public sales—such as in-store scales—must be tested, adjusted, and sealed annually by a municipal Sealer of Weights and Measures or a state compliance officer. Municipalities over 20,000 residents must appoint their own Sealer; the state Division of Standards covers roughly half of the 351 cities and towns. Unsealed or inaccurate devices may be condemned.

regulationName: M.G.L. c. 200A § 5D — Gift Certificate Validity, 7-Year Minimum & Cash Redemption
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州第200A章第5D条——礼品券有效期（7年下限）与现金兑付
englishTitle: MA Gift Certificate Law — 7-Year Validity & Cash-Out (Ch. 200A §5D)
agency: Massachusetts Office of the Attorney General / Office of Consumer Affairs and Business Regulation
applicabilityThreshold: Any seller of gift certificates/cards to MA consumers. 向马萨诸塞州消费者销售礼品券/卡的任何卖方。
keyRequirements: Gift certificates must be valid for at least 7 years from issuance with issuance and expiration dates disclosed, and once redeemed to 90% or more of value the consumer may elect to receive the remaining balance in cash.
complaintEnforcementRecord: MA OCABR/AG consumer guides on gift cards explain the 7-year minimum and the 90%-cash-out right as enforced consumer rights under §5D.
recommendedAction: Configure gift-card systems for a 7-year (or no) expiration and enable cash-out of balances once 90% is redeemed. 将礼品卡系统设为7年（或无）有效期，并在已兑付90%后支持余额现金兑付。
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州第200A章第5D条规定，出售的礼品券自发行之日起至少7年内有效，且发行日期和到期日须在券面（或电子卡的销售收据/网站/免费电话）上清晰标明；若未提供到期信息，则该券须永久可兑。当礼品券已兑付其价值的90%或以上时，消费者可选择以现金领取剩余余额。出售有效期少于7年的礼品券，可处不超过300美元的罚款。
englishSummary: M.G.L. c. 200A §5D requires that gift certificates sold be valid for at least 7 years from issuance, with the issuance and expiration dates clearly identified (on the face, or for electronic cards via receipt/website/toll-free number); if expiration info is not provided, the certificate is redeemable in perpetuity. Once a certificate is redeemed to 90% or more of its value, the consumer may elect to take the remaining balance in cash. Selling a certificate with a term under 7 years is punishable by a fine up to $300.

regulationName: M.G.L. c. 140D § 28A — Prohibition on Credit Card Surcharges
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州第140D章第28A条——禁止信用卡附加费
englishTitle: MA Credit Card Surcharge Ban (Ch. 140D §28A)
agency: Massachusetts Division of Banks / Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: Any seller accepting credit cards in MA. 在马萨诸塞州接受信用卡的任何卖方。
keyRequirements: No seller may impose a surcharge on a cardholder who elects to pay by credit card instead of cash, check, or similar means, though sellers may offer a discount for cash.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The MA Division of Banks has issued opinions (e.g., Opinion 21-005) narrowly construing when a third-party 'convenience fee' is permissible, reaffirming the §28A surcharge ban.
recommendedAction: Remove any credit-card surcharge in MA locations; if differential pricing is desired, frame it as a cash discount instead. 取消马萨诸塞州门店的信用卡附加费；如需差别定价，应改为现金折扣形式。
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州第140D章第28A条规定，任何卖方不得对选择以信用卡（而非现金、支票或类似方式）付款的持卡人收取附加费。马萨诸塞州是少数维持信用卡附加费全面禁令的州之一；任何专门针对信用卡使用而收取的额外费用都构成非法附加费。卖方可以为现金付款提供折扣，但不能对信用卡付款加价。
englishSummary: M.G.L. c. 140D §28A prohibits any seller from imposing a surcharge on a cardholder who chooses to pay by credit card instead of cash, check, or similar means. Massachusetts is one of only a handful of states maintaining an outright surcharge ban; any extra fee charged specifically for credit-card use is an illegal surcharge. Sellers may offer a cash discount but may not add a fee for paying by card. Narrow third-party 'convenience fee' exceptions exist only under strict Division of Banks criteria.

regulationName: 940 CMR 7.00 — Debt Collection Regulations
chineseTitle: 940 CMR 7.00——债务催收法规
englishTitle: 940 CMR 7.00 — Debt Collection Rules
agency: Massachusetts Office of the Attorney General
