applicabilityThreshold: Creditors and debt collectors collecting consumer debts in MA. 在马萨诸塞州催收消费者债务的债权人和催收方。
keyRequirements: Defines unfair or deceptive debt-collection acts by both creditors and third-party collectors, including limits on contact frequency (generally no more than twice per week by phone) and bars on false or misleading representations about a debt.
recommendedAction: If collecting on overdue accounts (e.g., catering), cap collection calls and keep all statements about the debt accurate and non-misleading. 若催收逾期账款（如餐饮服务），限制催收电话频率并确保所有债务陈述准确无误导。
chineseSummary: 940 CMR 7.00通过界定不公平或欺骗性的催收行为，为在马萨诸塞州向个人催收债务设定标准。该法规同时约束债权人和第三方催收方，禁止的行为包括：每周电话联系债务人超过两次、就债务的性质/金额/法律状态作出虚假或误导性陈述、以及谎称催收方为律师等。违反该法规即构成对93A章的违反。
englishSummary: 940 CMR 7.00 sets standards for collecting debts from persons in Massachusetts by defining unfair or deceptive collection acts. It applies to both creditors and third-party collectors and prohibits, among other things, initiating phone contact with a debtor more than twice per week, making knowingly false or misleading representations about the character/amount/legal status of a debt, and falsely implying the collector is an attorney. A violation is itself a violation of Chapter 93A.

regulationName: M.G.L. c. 93H & 201 CMR 17.00 — Data Breach Notification & Written Information Security Program
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州第93H章及201 CMR 17.00——数据泄露通知与书面信息安全计划
englishTitle: MA Data Security Law — Breach Notice & WISP (Ch. 93H / 201 CMR 17.00)
agency: Massachusetts Office of Consumer Affairs and Business Regulation / Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: Any person/entity that owns or licenses personal information of one or more MA residents. 拥有或许可使用一名或多名马萨诸塞州居民个人信息的任何主体。
keyRequirements: Any entity owning or licensing MA residents' personal information must maintain a comprehensive Written Information Security Program (WISP) and, upon a breach, notify affected residents and regulators as soon as practicable, offering at least 18 months of free credit monitoring if Social Security numbers are exposed.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The MA AG maintains a public data-breach reporting portal and database; breaches affecting MA residents must be reported and are routinely published and investigated.
recommendedAction: Adopt and maintain a documented WISP, assign an owner, and prepare a breach-response/notification plan including credit-monitoring offers for SSN exposure. 制定并维护书面信息安全计划（WISP），指定负责人，并准备含信用监控的泄露响应/通知方案。
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州第93H章及其配套法规201 CMR 17.00（2010年3月1日生效）共同构成两层义务：在泄露发生前主动保护个人信息，并在泄露发生后严格履行通报义务。任何拥有或许可使用马萨诸塞州居民个人信息的主体，必须制定、实施并维护与其规模相称的综合性书面信息安全计划（WISP）；发生泄露时须"尽快且无不合理延迟"通知受影响居民及监管机构，若涉及社会安全号，须提供至少18个月的免费信用监控。
englishSummary: M.G.L. c. 93H and its implementing regulation 201 CMR 17.00 (effective March 1, 2010) create a two-pronged duty: protect personal information proactively and notify after a breach. Any entity that owns or licenses personal information of MA residents must develop, implement, and maintain a comprehensive Written Information Security Program (WISP) proportional to its size and data. On a breach, it must notify affected residents and the AG/OCABR as soon as practicable, and offer at least 18 months of free credit monitoring if Social Security numbers were exposed.

regulationName: Massachusetts Data Privacy Act (H.5479 / S.2619) — Comprehensive Consumer Data Privacy (Pending)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州数据隐私法（H.5479 / S.2619）——综合性消费者数据隐私（待立法）
englishTitle: MA Data Privacy Act (H.5479 / S.2619) — Pending Comprehensive Privacy Law
agency: Massachusetts Legislature (enforcement by Office of the Attorney General if enacted)
applicabilityThreshold: Would apply to entities processing personal data of MA residents above thresholds set in the final bill (still being reconciled). 将适用于处理马萨诸塞州居民个人数据并达到最终法案所设门槛的主体（门槛仍在协调中）。
keyRequirements: Would grant MA residents rights to know, access, correct, delete, and port their personal data and to opt out of targeted advertising and sale of personal data, with heightened protections for minors and a ban on sale of precise geolocation data.
complaintEnforcementRecord: Senate Press Room and Foley Hoag analyses (June 2026) confirm the bill's passage in each chamber and its current conference-committee status pending final reconciliation and the Governor's signature.
recommendedAction: Monitor the conference-committee outcome; inventory customer data flows and pre-plan opt-out/consent mechanisms so compliance is ready if the Act is signed. 跟踪联席委员会结果，盘点客户数据流并预先规划退出/同意机制以备法案签署。
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州数据隐私法是一项综合性消费者隐私立法，截至2026年6月仍处于待立法状态、尚未签署成为法律。参议院于2025年9月25日以40比0一致通过（S.2619），众议院于2026年6月4日一致通过其修订版本（H.5479）；两院已成立联席会议委员会协调差异。该法将赋予居民知情、访问、更正、删除、可携带及退出定向广告/数据出售的权利，并对未成年人提供强化保护、全面禁止出售精确地理位置数据。
englishSummary: The Massachusetts Data Privacy Act is comprehensive consumer-privacy legislation that, as of June 2026, remains pending and not yet signed into law. The Senate passed it 40-0 on September 25, 2025 (S.2619) and the House unanimously passed an amended version (H.5479) on June 4, 2026; a House-Senate conference committee has been appointed to reconcile differences (e.g., enforcement model, cure periods, sensitive-data sales). It would give residents rights to know, access, correct, delete, and port personal data, opt out of targeted advertising and data sales, with strong minor protections and a ban on selling precise geolocation data.

regulationName: Florida Statutes § 501.142 — Retail Sales Establishments; Notice of Refund Policy
chineseTitle: 佛州零售退款政策告示法（§501.142）
englishTitle: Florida Refund Policy Posting Law (§ 501.142)
agency: Florida Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services (FDACS)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to retail sales establishments selling non-exempt goods to the public; does not apply to food/perishable sales. 适用于向公众销售非豁免商品的零售店。
keyRequirements: A retail establishment that gives no cash refund, credit refund, or merchandise exchange must post a sign so stating at the point of sale; if no such sign is posted, a refund/exchange policy is deemed to exist and the merchant must grant a refund within 7 days of purchase on unused merchandise in its original carton upon request and proof of purchase.
complaintEnforcementRecord: Enforcement is preempted to FDACS, which fields consumer refund complaints through its 1-800-HELP-FLA consumer hotline; failure-to-post triggers the statutory 7-day refund remedy.
recommendedAction: Post a compliant refund-policy sign at each Florida register; confirm food/custom-item exemptions are documented. 在佛州各收银点张贴合规退款政策告示。
chineseSummary: 佛州法律规定，凡不提供现金退款、信用退款或换货的零售店，必须在收银点张贴明确告示。若未张贴该‘不退款’告示，则视为存在退款/换货政策，须依消费者要求在购买后7天内对未使用且保留原包装的商品退款。该规定不适用于食品、易腐品及定制商品。
englishSummary: Florida requires any retail establishment that offers no cash refund, credit refund, or exchange to post a sign saying so at the point of sale. If the sign is not posted, the law presumes a refund/exchange policy exists, and the merchant must refund unused merchandise (in its original carton) within 7 days of purchase upon request and proof of purchase. The rule expressly exempts food, perishable goods, and custom-made items.

regulationName: Florida Deceptive and Unfair Trade Practices Act (FDUTPA), Fla. Stat. ch. 501 part II (§ 501.204)
chineseTitle: 佛州欺骗性及不公平交易行为法（FDUTPA）
englishTitle: Florida Deceptive and Unfair Trade Practices Act (FDUTPA)
agency: Florida Department of Legal Affairs (Office of the Attorney General) / State Attorneys
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to any person engaged in trade or commerce in Florida; no size threshold. 适用于在佛州从事贸易或商业的任何主体，无规模门槛。
keyRequirements: Section 501.204 broadly prohibits unfair methods of competition, unconscionable acts, and unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any trade or commerce, with civil penalties up to $10,000 per violation ($15,000 if a senior citizen is victimized) under § 501.2075.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The Florida AG routinely brings FDUTPA actions against retailers and chains for deceptive pricing and advertising; consumers file FDUTPA complaints via the AG and FDACS, and private FDUTPA class actions over fees are common in Florida courts.
recommendedAction: Run all FL menus, ads, app terms, and fee disclosures through a UDAP/substantiation review before launch. 上线前对佛州所有菜单、广告、App条款及费用披露进行UDAP审查。
chineseSummary: FDUTPA（佛州‘小型FTC法’）广泛禁止在任何商业活动中采用不公平竞争手段、不合理行为以及不公平或欺骗性行为。由佛州司法事务部（总检察长办公室）及州检察官执法，每项违规最高可处1万美元民事罚款，受害者为老年人时可达1.5万美元，并可要求消费者赔偿与禁令救济。
englishSummary: FDUTPA is Florida's 'Little FTC Act,' broadly outlawing unfair methods of competition, unconscionable acts, and unfair or deceptive acts in any trade or commerce. It is enforced by the Department of Legal Affairs and state attorneys, who can seek civil penalties up to $10,000 per violation (up to $15,000 where a senior citizen is victimized), restitution, and injunctions; consumers also have a private right of action for actual damages plus fees.

regulationName: Florida Statutes § 501.0117 — Credit Card Surcharge Prohibition (held unconstitutional, Dana's R.R. Supply v. AG, 11th Cir. 2015)
chineseTitle: 佛州信用卡附加费禁令（§501.0117，已被判违宪）
englishTitle: Florida Credit-Card Surcharge Ban (§ 501.0117 — Struck Down)
agency: Florida Department of Legal Affairs (Office of the Attorney General)
applicabilityThreshold: Nominally applies to sellers in credit-card sales transactions in Florida; unenforceable post-2015. 名义上适用于佛州信用卡销售交易的卖方；2015年后不可执行。
keyRequirements: The statute text still prohibits sellers from imposing a surcharge on customers who pay by credit card, but the Eleventh Circuit held it unconstitutional as a First Amendment speech violation in Dana's Railroad Supply v. Attorney General (2015), leaving it unenforceable.
complaintEnforcementRecord: After cease-and-desist letters to four businesses, those merchants sued and won in Dana's Railroad Supply v. Attorney General (11th Cir. 2015); Florida has not enforced § 501.0117 against credit-card surcharges since.
recommendedAction: Treat § 501.0117 as unenforceable; instead ensure any FL credit-card surcharge is conspicuously disclosed and within card-network caps. 视该条为不可执行；确保佛州信用卡附加费已醒目披露并符合卡组织上限。
chineseSummary: 佛州法规§501.0117表面上禁止商家对使用信用卡付款的顾客收取附加费。但2015年第十一巡回上诉法院在Dana's Railroad Supply案中判定该条款违反第一修正案言论自由，故实际不可执行。商家在佛州现可对信用卡交易加收附加费，但仍须清晰、真实地披露费用以免触犯FDUTPA。
englishSummary: Florida § 501.0117 nominally bans sellers from surcharging customers who pay by credit card. The Eleventh Circuit struck it down as an unconstitutional speech restriction in Dana's Railroad Supply v. Attorney General (2015), so it is unenforceable and remains on the books only as dead letter. Merchants may surcharge credit-card transactions in Florida, but the surcharge must still be clearly and truthfully disclosed to avoid FDUTPA liability.

regulationName: Florida Information Protection Act (FIPA), Fla. Stat. § 501.171 (personal-information definition amendment, ch. 2023-201)
chineseTitle: 佛州信息保护法（FIPA，§501.171）
englishTitle: Florida Information Protection Act (FIPA, § 501.171)
agency: Florida Department of Legal Affairs (Office of the Attorney General)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to commercial entities that acquire, maintain, or use personal information of Florida residents. 适用于获取、保存或使用佛州居民个人信息的商业主体。
keyRequirements: Covered entities must notify affected Florida residents of a data breach without unreasonable delay and no later than 30 days after determination, notify the Department of Legal Affairs if 500+ residents are affected, and (per ch. 2023-201, effective July 1, 2024) protect an expanded definition of 'personal information' that now includes biometric and geolocation data.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The Florida AG (Department of Legal Affairs) receives mandatory breach reports for incidents affecting 500+ residents and has pursued FIPA-related enforcement under the § 501.207 unfair-practices framework.
recommendedAction: Map FL customer/loyalty data flows, add biometric/geolocation to the breach inventory, and pre-build a 30-day breach-notification runbook. 梳理佛州客户与会员数据流，将生物识别/地理位置纳入泄露清单并预建30天通知预案。
chineseSummary: FIPA要求处理个人信息的主体在确定数据泄露后，无不合理延迟且不迟于30天内通知受影响的佛州居民；若涉及500名及以上居民，还须通知司法事务部。2023-201号法（2024年7月1日生效）扩大了‘个人信息’定义，纳入生物识别和地理位置数据。违规按不公平/欺骗行为处理，民事罚款最高50万美元。
englishSummary: FIPA requires any business holding personal information of Florida residents to notify affected individuals of a breach without unreasonable delay and no later than 30 days, and to notify the Department of Legal Affairs when 500 or more residents are affected. Chapter 2023-201 (effective July 1, 2024) expanded the definition of 'personal information' to cover biometric and geolocation data. Violations are treated as unfair/deceptive practices with civil penalties up to $500,000.

regulationName: Florida Statutes § 501.95 — Gift Certificates and Credit Memos (no expiration / no fees)
chineseTitle: 佛州礼品券与赊欠凭证法（§501.95）
englishTitle: Florida Gift Certificate Law (§ 501.95)
agency: Florida Department of Legal Affairs (Office of the Attorney General)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to gift certificates and credit memos sold or issued in Florida; narrow promotional/charitable/bank exceptions. 适用于在佛州销售或发行的礼品券与赊欠凭证；促销/慈善/银行类有限例外。
keyRequirements: A gift certificate or credit memo sold or issued in Florida may not carry an expiration date, expiration period, or any post-sale charge or fee (including service, dormancy, account-maintenance, or cash-out fees), subject to narrow exceptions for charitable, employee-incentive, loyalty/promotional, and certain bank-issued multi-merchant cards.
recommendedAction: Remove expiration dates and all post-sale fees from FL-sold gift cards; document any loyalty/promo exception relied upon. 取消佛州销售礼品卡的有效期与售后费用，记录所依赖的忠诚/促销例外。
chineseSummary: 佛州法律规定，在佛州销售或发行的礼品券/赊欠凭证不得设有效期或到期期限，也不得收取任何售后费用（包括服务费、休眠费、账户维护费或兑现费）。仅对慈善、员工激励、忠诚/促销赠券及部分银行发行的多商户卡设有限例外。违规可构成FDUTPA下的不公平行为。
englishSummary: Florida prohibits any gift certificate or credit memo sold or issued in the state from having an expiration date/period or any post-sale charge or fee, such as service, dormancy, account-maintenance, or cash-out fees. Limited exceptions cover charitable, employee-incentive, and loyalty/promotional certificates and certain bank-issued multi-merchant cards. Non-compliance can be treated as an unfair practice under FDUTPA.

regulationName: Florida Telephone Solicitation Act (FTSA) amendment, Fla. Stat. § 501.059 (SB 1120, 2021)
chineseTitle: 佛州电话营销法修正（FTSA，§501.059，2021年SB 1120）
englishTitle: Florida Telephone Solicitation Act Amendment (§ 501.059, 2021)
agency: Florida Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services (FDACS) / private right of action
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to telephonic sales calls (including texts) to Florida numbers using an automated dialing/selection system. 适用于使用自动拨号/选号系统向佛州号码发起的电话销售（含短信）。
keyRequirements: As amended by SB 1120 (effective July 1, 2021), the FTSA requires prior express written consent before making telephonic sales calls (including texts and voicemails) using an automated system to select or dial numbers, and honors the state Do Not Call list, with a private right of action allowing statutory damages of $500 (up to $1,500 for willful violations).
complaintEnforcementRecord: Since July 2021 the FTSA has driven a large wave of putative class actions in Florida federal and state courts over automated marketing texts, with the 2023 HB 761 amendments partly responding to that litigation surge.
recommendedAction: Audit FL SMS/marketing-call consent capture for 'prior express written consent' and scrub against the FL Do Not Call list. 审查佛州短信/营销电话的‘事先明确书面同意’采集，并比对谢绝来电名单。
chineseSummary: 2021年SB 1120修正后的FTSA规定，使用自动系统选择或拨打号码进行电话销售（含短信、语音留言）前，须取得消费者事先明确的书面同意，并遵守佛州谢绝来电名单。该法赋予私人诉权，每次违规法定赔偿500美元，故意违规最高1500美元，2023年HB 761又作收窄修订。
englishSummary: SB 1120 (effective July 1, 2021) amended Florida's Telephone Solicitation Act to require prior express written consent before placing telephonic sales calls — including texts and prerecorded/voicemail messages — using an automated system to select or dial numbers, and to honor the Florida Do Not Call list. It created a private right of action with statutory damages of $500 per violation (up to $1,500 for willful violations); HB 761 (2023) later narrowed some provisions.

regulationName: DC Consumer Protection Procedures Act (CPPA), D.C. Code § 28-3904
chineseTitle: 华盛顿特区消费者保护程序法（CPPA，§28-3904）
englishTitle: DC Consumer Protection Procedures Act (§ 28-3904)
agency: DC Office of the Attorney General (OAG); DC Department of Licensing and Consumer Protection (DLCP)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to any merchant offering or selling consumer goods or services in DC; no size threshold. 适用于在特区提供或销售消费品/服务的任何商家，无规模门槛。
keyRequirements: Section 28-3904 makes it unlawful to engage in any of ~38 enumerated unfair or deceptive trade practices — including misrepresenting goods/services, false or misleading price representations, failing to disclose material facts, and using unconscionable terms — whether or not any consumer is in fact misled or damaged.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The DC OAG regularly sues businesses under the CPPA for deceptive pricing and fees, and consumers file CPPA actions in DC Superior Court; the OAG's restaurant-fee enforcement program is built on § 28-3904.
recommendedAction: Treat the CPPA as DC's master UDAP rule — pre-clear all DC pricing, ads, and fee disclosures against its 38 enumerated practices. 将CPPA视为特区UDAP主规则，对所有定价、广告及费用披露事先审查。
chineseSummary: 特区CPPA第28-3904条列举了约38类不公平或欺骗性交易行为，并将其全部列为违法，无论消费者是否实际被误导或受损。涵盖虚假陈述商品/服务、虚假或误导性价格表示、隐瞒重大事实及不合理合同条款等。由特区总检察长办公室执法，消费者享私人诉权（§28-3905(k)），可获三倍损害赔偿或每次违规1500美元（取较高者）及律师费。
englishSummary: The CPPA's § 28-3904 declares roughly 38 enumerated unfair or deceptive trade practices unlawful — including misrepresenting goods or services, false or misleading price representations, failing to disclose material facts, and unconscionable terms — regardless of whether any consumer was actually misled or harmed. It is enforced by the DC OAG (and DLCP), and consumers have a private right of action (§ 28-3905(k)) for treble damages or $1,500 per violation (whichever is greater) plus attorney's fees.

regulationName: DC OAG Business Advisory on Restaurant Service-Fee Disclosure (CPPA enforcement guidance, Aug. 9, 2023 + supplemental)
chineseTitle: 特区总检察长餐厅服务费披露指引（CPPA，2023年8月）
englishTitle: DC OAG Restaurant Service-Fee Disclosure Advisory (2023)
agency: DC Office of the Attorney General (OAG)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to DC food-service establishments charging any mandatory fee to customers. 适用于向顾客收取任何强制费用的特区餐饮场所。
keyRequirements: Under the CPPA, restaurants must disclose all mandatory fees (service, wellness, recovery fees, etc.) to diners in a timely, prominent, and accurate manner before they order — via menu, signage, and/or orally — and must accurately describe the fee's purpose, including how much goes to base operating costs versus worker compensation.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The OAG launched the advisory after numerous consumer complaints about undisclosed 'service fees'; in 2024 it secured $126,650 for 42 hotel-restaurant workers (former Moon Rabbit / IHG) over mishandled service-fee and tip disclosures.
recommendedAction: Disclose any DC mandatory fee and its exact purpose on menus/signage before order; avoid generic 'service fee' labels. 在特区点单前于菜单/告示披露任何强制费用及其确切用途，避免笼统标注‘服务费’。
chineseSummary: 特区总检察长于2023年8月9日发布并随后补充的商业指引明确：依据CPPA，餐厅必须在顾客点单前，以及时、显著、准确的方式（菜单、告示和/或口头）披露所有强制性费用（服务费、‘健康费’、‘恢复费’等），并准确说明费用用途，包括用于基本运营成本与员工报酬的比例。隐藏费用或含糊标注‘服务费’属违法。
englishSummary: The DC OAG's August 9, 2023 advisory (and a later supplemental advisory) makes clear that under the CPPA, restaurants must disclose all mandatory fees — service, 'wellness,' and 'recovery' fees — to diners in a timely, prominent, and accurate way before ordering, through the menu, signage, and/or oral notice. The advisory requires restaurants to accurately describe each fee's purpose, including the share used for base operating costs versus worker compensation, and lists compliant vs. non-compliant disclosure examples.

regulationName: DC Basic Business License & Food Establishment License (D.C. Official Code Title 47, ch. 28; DCMR Title 25)
chineseTitle: 特区基本营业执照与食品经营许可证
englishTitle: DC Basic Business License & Food Establishment License
agency: DC Department of Licensing and Consumer Protection (DLCP)
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to every business location operating or serving food in the District of Columbia. 适用于在特区运营或提供食品的每个营业地点。
keyRequirements: A food-serving business in DC must obtain a Basic Business License (BBL) in the appropriate endorsement category from DLCP plus a Food Establishment license, supported by a Certificate of Occupancy, an approved DC Health inspection, and DC sales-and-use tax registration before operating.
complaintEnforcementRecord: DLCP routinely cites and fines DC food establishments operating without a valid BBL or food license and can order unlicensed operations to cease.
recommendedAction: Build BBL + Food Establishment license, C of O, and health-inspection sign-off into the DC store-opening checklist and track renewal dates. 将BBL+食品经营许可证、占用证明及卫生检查纳入特区开店清单并跟踪续期。
chineseSummary: 在特区经营咖啡馆/餐饮，须先向DLCP取得相应类别的基本营业执照（BBL）及食品经营许可证，并提交占用证明、通过DC Health卫生检查、完成销售与使用税登记。无证经营或许可过期将面临罚款及停业。许可证须定期续期。
englishSummary: Any business preparing or serving food in DC must obtain a Basic Business License (BBL) in the proper endorsement category from DLCP, plus a Food Establishment license, supported by a Certificate of Occupancy, a passed DC Health inspection, and DC sales-and-use tax registration before opening. Operating unlicensed or with an expired license exposes the business to fines and closure; licenses must be renewed periodically.

regulationName: DC Weights & Measures Device Registration & Inspection (D.C. Code Title 37, ch. 2; § 37-201.33)
chineseTitle: 特区计量器具登记与检定（D.C. Code 第37编）
englishTitle: DC Weights & Measures Device Registration (Title 37)
agency: DC Department of Licensing and Consumer Protection (DLCP), Office of Weights and Measures
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to DC businesses using commercial weighing or measuring devices (scales, UPC scanners). 适用于在特区使用商用称量/计量器具（秤、UPC扫描器）的企业。
keyRequirements: Businesses must register and annually renew all commercially used weighing and measuring devices — including UPC/price scanners and produce/retail scales — with DLCP's Office of Weights and Measures, which inspects devices for accuracy (the OWM inspects twice yearly), with fines of about $2,034 per violation for using unregistered or uninspected devices.
complaintEnforcementRecord: DLCP OWM inspects thousands of DC devices twice yearly and assesses per-device fines for unregistered/uninspected equipment; it also runs annual Consumer Protection and Weights & Measures outreach events.
recommendedAction: Inventory DC scales/scanners, register them with DLCP OWM, and calendar the October 1 annual renewal. 盘点特区秤具/扫描器，向DLCP计量办公室登记，并设定10月1日年度续期提醒。
chineseSummary: 依特区第37编，商家须向DLCP计量办公室登记并每年续期所有商用称量与计量器具，包括UPC/价格扫描器及零售/果蔬秤；计量办公室每年检定两次以确保准确。使用未登记或未检定器具，每项违规约罚2,034美元，逾10月1日续期另加250美元滞纳金。登记周期为10月1日至次年9月30日。
englishSummary: Under DC Title 37, businesses must register and annually renew all commercially used weighing and measuring devices — including UPC/price scanners and retail/produce scales — with DLCP's Office of Weights and Measures, which inspects them for accuracy roughly twice a year. Using an unregistered or uninspected device carries a fine of about $2,034 per violation, plus a $250 late fee for missing the October 1 renewal deadline.

regulationName: Workers and Restaurants Are Priorities Act of 2023 (DC Bill 25-280) — mandatory-fee disclosure provisions
chineseTitle: 《2023年员工与餐厅优先法》（特区第25-280号法案）
englishTitle: Workers and Restaurants Are Priorities Act of 2023 (DC Bill 25-280)
agency: Council of the District of Columbia / DC Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: Would have applied to DC restaurants/food-service establishments charging mandatory fees, if enacted; bill did not pass. 若获通过，将适用于收取强制费用的特区餐饮场所；该法案未通过。
keyRequirements: Section 3 of the bill addressed restaurants' mandatory service/operating-fee disclosure (allowing disclosure via verbal notice, signage, or website) and Section 4 proposed a tax exemption for service charges up to 22%; the OAG opposed both as weakening existing CPPA fee-disclosure protections. The bill was not enacted under this number during Council Period 25 (2023-2024) and is no longer active.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The bill arose from a surge of consumer complaints and advocacy (e.g., Travelers United) over undisclosed DC restaurant 'service fees'; the OAG submitted formal testimony opposing Sections 3 and 4, and the measure was not enacted under this number.
recommendedAction: Treat Bill 25-280 as dead (not enacted in Council Period 25) but monitor for reintroduction; meanwhile comply with the operative CPPA fee-disclosure advisory. 视第25-280号法案为已失效（第25届会期未通过），但关注其再次提出的可能；同时遵守现行CPPA费用披露指引。
chineseSummary: 特区第25-280号法案曾拟调整餐厅强制费用披露规则：第3节允许餐厅以口头、显著告示或网站方式披露强制性服务费/运营费，第4节拟对最高22%的服务费给予税收豁免。总检察长办公室就第3、4节提出反对，认为其削弱现行CPPA对消费者与员工的保护。该法案在第25届议会（2023-2024年）会期内未以此编号通过，现已失效；相关服务费议题由CPPA指引及2024年餐厅复兴法（D.C. Law 25-162）另行推进，仍值得持续关注。
englishSummary: DC Bill 25-280 would have revised restaurants' mandatory-fee disclosure duties — Section 3 would have permitted disclosure via verbal notice, signage, or website (rather than prominent upfront disclosure), and Section 4 proposed a tax exemption for service charges up to 22%. The OAG testified against Sections 3 and 4 as weakening existing CPPA consumer/worker protections. The bill was NOT enacted under this number and died at the end of DC Council Period 25 (2023-2024); related service-charge provisions advanced separately via the 2024 Restaurant Revitalization Act (D.C. Law 25-162). The CPPA advisory remains the operative fee-disclosure rule, so the topic stays worth monitoring for reintroduction.

regulationName: DC Uniform Disposition of Unclaimed Property Act — Gift Cards Presumed Abandoned, D.C. Code § 41-152.07
chineseTitle: 特区无人认领财产法——礼品卡推定遗弃（§41-152.07）
englishTitle: DC Unclaimed Property — Gift Card Abandonment (§ 41-152.07)
agency: DC Office of the Chief Financial Officer, Unclaimed Property Unit
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to gift-card balances attributable to DC and reportable under DC unclaimed-property rules. 适用于归属特区且须按特区无人认领财产规则申报的礼品卡余额。
keyRequirements: A gift card is presumed abandoned in DC if it is unclaimed by the apparent owner 5 years after the later of the date of purchase or its most recent use, at which point the unredeemed value becomes reportable/remittable to the District as unclaimed property.
recommendedAction: Add a 5-year dormancy track for DC gift-card balances and an annual escheat-reporting step to the OCFO Unclaimed Property Unit. 为特区礼品卡余额设置5年休眠跟踪，并将年度上缴申报纳入财务流程。
chineseSummary: 根据特区《统一无人认领财产法》（D.C. Law 24-45，2021年11月13日生效）第41-152.07条，礼品卡若在购买日或最近一次使用日（以较晚者为准）后满5年仍未被认领，即推定为遗弃财产，其未兑付余额须向特区申报并上缴。这影响礼品卡余额的会计处理与上缴义务。
englishSummary: Under DC's Uniform Disposition of Unclaimed Property Act (D.C. Law 24-45, effective Nov. 13, 2021), § 41-152.07 provides that a gift card is presumed abandoned if unclaimed 5 years after the later of its purchase date or most recent use. At that point the unredeemed value is reportable and remittable to the District as unclaimed property, affecting how a merchant accounts for and escheats outstanding gift-card balances.

regulationName: Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA), 47 U.S.C. § 227 — and FCC 2025 Consent-Revocation Rule (47 CFR 64.1200)
chineseTitle: 《电话消费者保护法》(TCPA)及FCC 2025年退订规则
englishTitle: TCPA — Marketing Calls/Texts Consent & 2025 Opt-Out Rule
agency: Federal Communications Commission (FCC); private right of action in federal/state courts
applicabilityThreshold: 任何向美国消费者拨打营销电话或发送营销短信/传真的商家 / Any business placing marketing calls or sending marketing texts/faxes to U.S. consumers.
keyRequirements: A café must obtain prior express written consent before sending marketing texts or robocalls to a customer's phone, and must honor opt-out/revocation requests made by any reasonable means within 10 business days.
complaintEnforcementRecord: TCPA generates among the highest volumes of federal consumer-protection class actions; settlements routinely run into the millions (e.g., multi-million-dollar settlements against national retailers over unconsented promotional texts).
recommendedAction: 采集营销短信/电话前留存可证明的事先明确书面同意,并建立10个工作日内执行的全渠道退订机制 / Capture documented written consent before any marketing text; build an all-channel opt-out workflow honored within 10 business days.
chineseSummary: TCPA(1991年颁布)禁止未经事先明确书面同意向消费者发送营销短信或拨打自动营销电话。FCC于2025年4月11日生效的新退订规则允许消费者以任何合理方式撤回同意,商家须在10个工作日内处理。法定赔偿为每次违规500至1500美元,且常引发集体诉讼。
englishSummary: The TCPA (enacted 1991) bars marketing texts and robocalls to consumers without prior express written consent. The FCC's revocation rule effective April 11, 2025 lets consumers opt out 'in any reasonable manner,' and businesses must honor it within 10 business days. Statutory damages are $500–$1,500 per message/call, frequently litigated as class actions. (Note: the related 'revoke-all' sub-provision, 47 CFR 64.1200(a)(10), was waived and is now delayed to Jan. 31, 2027; the core revocation and 10-business-day rule are in effect.)

regulationName: New York General Business Law § 396-r — Price Gouging (and OAG Final Rules / new Proposed Rule, Jan. 28, 2026)
chineseTitle: 纽约州《一般商业法》第396-r条——价格欺诈(暴利)
englishTitle: NY GBL § 396-r — Price Gouging Prohibition
agency: New York State Office of the Attorney General (OAG)
applicabilityThreshold: 在纽约州销售消费品/服务的所有卖家,涵盖供应链各环节 / All sellers of consumer goods/services in NY, across the distribution chain.
keyRequirements: During any 'abnormal disruption of the market' (e.g., severe weather, power failure, or a Governor-declared state of emergency), a seller may not charge an 'unconscionably excessive price' for consumer goods or services.
complaintEnforcementRecord: OAG has repeatedly issued price-gouging consumer alerts and pursued enforcement after events such as Hurricane Henri (2021) and during the COVID-19 emergency, the latter prompting the 2026 rulemaking.
recommendedAction: 在任何纽约州宣布的紧急状态期间冻结价格或留存成本上涨凭证,确保涨幅不超过10% / Freeze prices or retain cost-increase documentation during any NY-declared emergency; keep increases at/under the new 10% gross-disparity trigger.
chineseSummary: GBL §396-r禁止在市场异常中断期间(如恶劣天气、停电或州长宣布紧急状态)以'不合理过高'的价格出售消费品或服务。2026年1月28日,纽约州总检察长定稿六项规则并提出一项新拟议规则,明确价格上涨超过10%即构成'明显差异',可触发执法;市场份额超30%的卖家任何涨价均推定违规。每次违规民事罚款最高2.5万美元或毛收入三倍(取较高者)。
englishSummary: GBL § 396-r bars 'unconscionably excessive' pricing of consumer goods/services during an abnormal market disruption such as severe weather, outages, or a Governor-declared emergency. On Jan. 28, 2026 the OAG finalized six rules and issued one new proposed rule, establishing that any price increase over 10% is a presumptive 'gross disparity,' with sellers holding >30% market share presumed in violation for any increase. Penalties reach $25,000 per violation or three times gross receipts, whichever is greater.

regulationName: Song-Beverly Credit Card Act — Civil Code § 1747.08 (Personal Identification Information at Point of Sale)
chineseTitle: 加州《Song-Beverly信用卡法》——民法典第1747.08条(POS个人身份信息)
englishTitle: CA Song-Beverly Act § 1747.08 — No PII Collection at Card POS
agency: California Attorney General; district/city attorneys; private right of action
applicabilityThreshold: 在加州实体门店接受信用卡的零售商(不含在线下载类交易及法定例外) / Retailers accepting credit cards at California brick-and-mortar POS (online-download sales and statutory exceptions excluded).
keyRequirements: A retailer may not request and record a cardholder's personal identification information (including ZIP code, address, or phone number) as a condition of a credit-card transaction.
complaintEnforcementRecord: Pineda v. Williams-Sonoma, 51 Cal.4th 524 (2011) confirmed ZIP-code collection is actionable; numerous retailers (e.g., national specialty and apparel chains) have since paid multimillion-dollar class settlements.
recommendedAction: 审核加州门店POS与App结账流程,刷卡付款时不得强制索取邮编/电话/邮箱;如需收集须在交易完成后且明确为自愿 / Audit CA POS/app checkout so ZIP/phone/email is never required at card payment; collect only after the sale and clearly as optional.
chineseSummary: 民法典§1747.08禁止零售商在信用卡交易中索取并记录持卡人个人身份信息,作为接受信用卡付款的条件。加州最高法院在2011年Pineda诉Williams-Sonoma案中裁定邮编(ZIP code)属于个人身份信息,引发大量集体诉讼。每次首违罚款最高250美元,后续每次最高1000美元。
englishSummary: Civil Code § 1747.08 prohibits a retailer from requesting and recording a cardholder's personal identification information — including ZIP code, address, or phone number — as a condition of accepting a credit card. In Pineda v. Williams-Sonoma (2011) the California Supreme Court held a ZIP code is PII, triggering a wave of class actions. Penalties are up to $250 for a first violation and $1,000 per subsequent violation.

regulationName: Truth-in-Consumer Contract, Warranty and Notice Act (TCCWNA), N.J.S.A. 56:12-14 to -18
chineseTitle: 新泽西州《真实消费者合同、保证与告示法》(TCCWNA)
englishTitle: NJ TCCWNA — Consumer Contracts, Notices & Signs
agency: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs; private right of action
applicabilityThreshold: 向新泽西消费者提供合同、保证、告示或标识的任何卖方 / Any seller giving NJ consumers a consumer contract, warranty, notice, or sign.
keyRequirements: A seller may not offer or display any consumer contract, warranty, notice, or sign that contains a provision violating a clearly established legal right of a consumer or responsibility of a seller under NJ or federal law.
complaintEnforcementRecord: TCCWNA drove a wave of NJ class actions over website terms and in-store signage (2015–2018); Spade v. Select Comfort Corp., 232 N.J. 504 (2018) narrowed standing by requiring proof of harm.
recommendedAction: 在新泽西开店前由法务审核所有店内标识、收据与App条款,删除违反消费者法定权利的免责或'无退款'措辞 / Before NJ entry, have counsel scrub all signage, receipts, and app terms of disclaimers or 'no refund' language that violates established consumer rights.
chineseSummary: TCCWNA(P.L.1981, c.454,1982年生效)禁止商家向消费者提供或展示任何含有违反消费者明确法定权利条款的合同、保证、告示或店内标识。值得注意的是,该法明确涵盖'告示与标识',如免责声明牌。违者须向受害消费者承担不少于100美元的民事罚款及实际损失,外加律师费。2018年Spade诉Select Comfort案要求原告须证明受到实际损害方为'受害消费者'。
englishSummary: The TCCWNA (P.L. 1981, c. 454, effective 1982) bars a seller from giving or displaying any consumer contract, warranty, notice, or sign containing a provision that violates a clearly established legal right of a consumer. Critically, it expressly reaches 'notices and signs' — such as in-store disclaimer placards. Violators owe an aggrieved consumer a civil penalty of at least $100 plus actual damages and attorney's fees. Spade v. Select Comfort (NJ 2018) clarified that a plaintiff must show actual harm to be 'aggrieved.'

regulationName: Unit Price Disclosure Act & Rules, N.J.S.A. 56:8-25 / N.J.A.C. 13:45A-14
chineseTitle: 新泽西州《单位价格披露法》及规章 (N.J.A.C. 13:45A-14)
englishTitle: NJ Unit Pricing of Consumer Commodities
agency: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs (Office of Weights and Measures)
applicabilityThreshold: 在新泽西零售受监管包装消费品且上一年此类毛收入超200万美元的门店 / NJ retailers of regulated packaged commodities with prior-year such receipts over $2M.
keyRequirements: Covered retailers must display the unit price (e.g., per ounce/pound) of regulated consumer commodities using an approved, consistent unit of measure so consumers can compare prices across package sizes.
recommendedAction: 对新泽西门店货架上的包装咖啡豆/瓶装饮料确认是否超出200万美元豁免线,如适用则加贴合规单位价格标签 / Check whether NJ shelf-sold packaged coffee/bottled drinks exceed the $2M exemption; if so, apply compliant unit-price labels.
chineseSummary: 新泽西《单位价格披露法》(N.J.S.A. 56:8-25)及实施规章N.J.A.C. 13:45A-14要求受监管的零售商以经核准且一致的计量单位(如每盎司、每磅)标示受监管消费品的单位价格,便于消费者比价。单一门店且上一年消费品销售毛收入不超过200万美元者可豁免。由消费者事务司度量衡办公室执法。
englishSummary: New Jersey's Unit Price Disclosure Act (N.J.S.A. 56:8-25) and rules at N.J.A.C. 13:45A-14 require covered retailers to display the unit price (per ounce/pound, etc.) of regulated consumer commodities using an approved, consistent unit of measure to enable easy price comparison. A single establishment with prior-year consumer-commodity gross receipts of $2 million or less is exempt. Enforced by the Division of Consumer Affairs' Office of Weights and Measures.

regulationName: Florida Game Promotion Act — Fla. Stat. § 849.094
chineseTitle: 佛州《促销游戏法》——佛州法典第849.094条
englishTitle: FL Game Promotion / Sweepstakes Registration Act
agency: Florida Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services (FDACS); Department of Legal Affairs
applicabilityThreshold: 在佛州开展、奖品总额超过5000美元的促销游戏/抽奖 / Game promotions/sweepstakes operated in FL with total prize value over $5,000.
keyRequirements: An operator running a sweepstakes or 'game promotion' tied to product sales with total prizes exceeding $5,000 must file the rules and a $100 fee with FDACS at least 7 days in advance, post a surety bond or trust account for the full prize value, and conspicuously post the rules at all retail locations.
complaintEnforcementRecord: FDACS routinely accepts and audits game-promotion filings and has pursued operators that failed to register or post bonds; non-registration is treated as a per-se FDUTPA violation.
recommendedAction: 佛州任何奖品总额超5000美元的促销抽奖,提前至少7天向FDACS备案规则、缴费并设立信托/保证金,门店显著张贴规则 / For any FL sweepstakes with prizes over $5,000, file rules and fee with FDACS at least 7 days ahead, post a trust/bond, and display rules in stores.
chineseSummary: 佛州法典§849.094规定,凡与产品/服务销售挂钩的抽奖或'促销游戏',若奖品总额超过5000美元,经营者须在活动开始前至少7天向农业与消费者服务部备案规则并缴纳100美元费用,按全部奖品价值设立信托账户或交付保证金,并在所有零售点显著张贴规则。禁止操纵结果、虚假宣传或任意取消参赛资格。违反构成二级轻罪及FDUTPA下的欺骗性不公平交易行为,民事罚款每次最高1000美元。
englishSummary: Fla. Stat. § 849.094 requires operators of sweepstakes or 'game promotions' tied to product sales with total prizes over $5,000 to file rules and a $100 fee with FDACS at least 7 days before launch, secure a trust account or surety bond for the full prize value, and conspicuously post rules at all retail locations. Rigging outcomes, false advertising, and arbitrary disqualification are prohibited. Violations are second-degree misdemeanors and deceptive/unfair trade practices under FDUTPA, with civil penalties up to $1,000 per violation.

regulationName: Massachusetts Do Not Call Law — M.G.L. c. 159C and 201 CMR 12.00
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州《免打扰名单法》(M.G.L. c.159C / 201 CMR 12.00)
englishTitle: MA Do-Not-Call Registry & Telephone Solicitation Rules
agency: Massachusetts Office of Consumer Affairs and Business Regulation (OCABR); Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: 向马萨诸塞消费者拨打主动推销电话的商家 / Businesses placing unsolicited sales calls to Massachusetts consumers.
keyRequirements: Telephone solicitors must register annually with OCABR, scrub against the quarterly Massachusetts Do Not Call Registry, and may not make unsolicited sales calls to listed consumers or to anyone between 8:00 p.m. and 8:00 a.m.
complaintEnforcementRecord: OCABR maintains the registry and refers violations to the Attorney General; the AG has obtained assurances of discontinuance and penalties against telemarketers ignoring the state list.
recommendedAction: 如对马萨诸塞消费者拨打推销电话,须每年向OCABR登记、按季度比对州免打扰名单并遵守8:00–20:00时段限制 / If calling MA consumers for sales, register annually with OCABR, scrub the state registry quarterly, and call only 8am–8pm.
chineseSummary: M.G.L. c.159C及201 CMR 12.00要求电话营销者每年向消费者事务与商业监管办公室登记,按季度比对马萨诸塞州免打扰名单,不得向名单内消费者拨打主动推销电话,也不得在晚8点至早8点拨打。违反构成93A章下的不公平或欺骗行为,可处民事罚款。
englishSummary: M.G.L. c. 159C and 201 CMR 12.00 require telephone solicitors to register annually with OCABR, scrub the quarterly Massachusetts Do Not Call Registry, and refrain from unsolicited sales calls to listed consumers or any calls between 8:00 p.m. and 8:00 a.m. Violations are unfair/deceptive acts under Chapter 93A and carry civil penalties.

regulationName: New Jersey Gift Card / Gift Certificate Law — N.J.S.A. 56:8-110
chineseTitle: 新泽西州《礼品卡/礼券法》(N.J.S.A. 56:8-110)
englishTitle: NJ Gift Card Law — Expiration, Fees & Cash-Back
agency: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs
applicabilityThreshold: 在新泽西出售或可赎回的礼品卡/礼券发行人 / Issuers of gift cards/certificates sold or redeemable in New Jersey.
keyRequirements: Gift card funds may not expire within 24 months of sale, no dormancy fee may be charged during the first 24 months (and any later fee is capped at $2/month), and a remaining balance under $5 must be refunded in cash on the holder's request.
recommendedAction: 确保新泽西销售的礼品卡24个月内不到期、不收休眠费,披露用≥10号字体,POS支持余额<5美元现金退还 / Ensure NJ gift cards have no <24-month expiration/fee, carry ≥10-pt disclosures, and the POS cashes out sub-$5 balances on request.
chineseSummary: N.J.S.A. 56:8-110规定礼品卡/礼券的底层资金在售出后24个月内不得到期,前24个月内不得收取休眠费(其后每月不超过2美元),相关到期或休眠费条款须以不小于10号字体在卡片、收据或包装上披露。当卡内余额低于5美元时,持有人有权要求以现金退还余额(自2012年9月1日起)。
englishSummary: N.J.S.A. 56:8-110 provides that gift card/certificate funds may not expire within 24 months of sale and bars dormancy fees during the first 24 months (later fees capped at $2/month), with any expiration/fee terms disclosed in at least 10-point font on the card, receipt, or package. When a redeemed card's remaining balance falls below $5, the holder may demand a cash refund of the balance (effective Sept. 1, 2012).

regulationName: Cashless Retailers Prohibition Amendment Act of 2020 — D.C. Law 23-187 (D.C. Code § 28-3825)
chineseTitle: 华盛顿特区《禁止无现金零售商修正法》(D.C. Law 23-187)
englishTitle: DC Cashless Retailers Prohibition Act
agency: DC Office of the Attorney General (enforced as an unlawful trade practice under § 28-3904)
applicabilityThreshold: 在华盛顿特区面向公众的实体零售场所(邮购/电话/网购及部分停车设施除外) / In-person public-facing retail establishments in DC (mail/phone/online and certain parking facilities excepted).
keyRequirements: A retail establishment serving the public may not refuse to accept cash, post signs stating cash is not accepted, or charge cash-paying customers a higher price than card-paying customers.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The District began actively enforcing the cashless ban on October 1, 2023, putting bars, restaurants, and retailers on notice that refusing cash or posting 'no cash' signs is an actionable unlawful trade practice.
recommendedAction: 确保特区门店全部接受现金、移除任何'仅限刷卡/无现金'标识,且现金与刷卡同价 / Ensure DC stores accept cash, remove any 'card only/cashless' signage, and price cash equal to card.
chineseSummary: D.C. Law 23-187禁止面向公众的零售场所拒收现金、张贴'不收现金'标识或对付现金的顾客收取更高价格。该法于2021年3月16日生效,特区于2023年10月1日起加强执法。违反构成§28-3904下的非法贸易行为,可处民事罚款,由特区总检察长执法。设有现金转预付卡设备(不收费、最低存额不超过5美元)等例外。
englishSummary: D.C. Law 23-187 bars public-facing retail establishments from refusing cash, posting signs that cash is not accepted, or charging cash payers a higher price than card payers. Effective March 16, 2021, with the District stepping up enforcement from October 1, 2023, violations are unlawful trade practices under § 28-3904 enforced by the DC OAG. Exceptions include on-site cash-to-prepaid-card machines charging no fee and no minimum over $5.

regulationName: Children's Online Privacy Protection Rule (COPPA Rule), 16 CFR Part 312 — 2025 Amendments
chineseTitle: 联邦《儿童在线隐私保护规则》(COPPA)——2025年修订
englishTitle: COPPA Rule (16 CFR 312) — 2025 Amendments
agency: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
applicabilityThreshold: 面向儿童或明知收集13岁以下儿童个人信息的在线服务运营者 / Operators of online services directed to children or that knowingly collect personal info from children under 13.
keyRequirements: An online service that knowingly collects personal information from children under 13 must obtain verifiable parental consent, must obtain separate consent before disclosing/monetizing that data via targeted advertising, and must meet new data-retention and security obligations.
complaintEnforcementRecord: The FTC has brought numerous COPPA enforcement actions with multimillion-dollar penalties (e.g., against major platforms and app makers) for collecting children's data without verifiable parental consent.
recommendedAction: 确认App/营销不针对或不故意收集13岁以下儿童数据;若涉及,须实施可验证家长同意与数据保留控制(合规期限2026年4月22日已届至) / Confirm the app/marketing does not target or knowingly collect under-13 data; if it does, implement verifiable parental consent and retention controls (the April 22, 2026 compliance deadline has now passed).
chineseSummary: FTC于2025年修订COPPA规则(16 CFR Part 312),规则于2025年6月23日生效,合规截止日为2026年4月22日(现已届至)。修订要求在线服务在向第三方披露或用于定向广告变现儿童(13岁以下)个人信息前取得单独可验证的家长同意,并新增数据保留与安全义务,以限制对儿童数据的货币化。由FTC执法。
englishSummary: The FTC's 2025 amendments to the COPPA Rule (16 CFR Part 312) became effective June 23, 2025, with a compliance deadline of April 22, 2026 (now passed). They require separate verifiable parental consent before disclosing or monetizing the personal information of children under 13 through targeted advertising, and add data-retention and security obligations limiting monetization of kids' data. Enforced by the FTC.

regulationName: NYC Refund Policy Posting Rule (6 RCNY §5-37)
chineseTitle: NYC 退款政策张贴规则（6 RCNY §5-37）
englishTitle: NYC Refund Policy Posting Rule
agency: NYC DCWP
applicabilityThreshold: 面向消费者销售商品的零售/餐饮场所
keyRequirements: 在店内显眼处张贴退款政策；未张贴则消费者可在 30 天内退货。
recommendedAction: 在各门店与点单界面显眼处张贴/展示退款政策。
chineseSummary: NYC DCWP 要求商家在显眼处张贴退款政策；若未张贴，消费者有权在购买后 30 天内退货退款。
englishSummary: NYC DCWP requires merchants to conspicuously post their refund policy; if none is posted, consumers may return goods within 30 days of purchase.

regulationName: NYC Consumer Protection Law — Pricing & Signage (6 RCNY Ch. 5)
chineseTitle: NYC 消费者保护法 —— 标价与告示
englishTitle: NYC Consumer Protection Law — Pricing & Signage
agency: NYC DCWP
applicabilityThreshold: 面向消费者的商户
keyRequirements: 明确标价、避免误导性定价与广告；遵守告示与单品标价规则。
complaintEnforcementRecord: DCWP 经常对零售/餐饮商户进行单品标价与告示检查并处罚。
recommendedAction: 核查门店与 App 的标价、促销与告示是否清晰、无误导。
chineseSummary: NYC 消费者保护法禁止不公平或误导性的商业行为，并就标价、告示与广告设定要求；DCWP 进行检查与执法。
englishSummary: NYC's Consumer Protection Law bars unfair/deceptive practices and sets pricing, signage and advertising requirements; DCWP inspects and enforces.

regulationName: FTC Act §5 — Unfair or Deceptive Acts or Practices
chineseTitle: FTC 法第 5 条 —— 不公平或欺骗性行为
englishTitle: FTC Act Section 5 — Unfair or Deceptive Acts or Practices
agency: FTC
applicabilityThreshold: 所有企业（联邦基线）
keyRequirements: 禁止不公平或欺骗性的商业行为，含广告、定价与订阅/会员条款。
recommendedAction: 审查 App 会员/订阅、促销与广告表述，确保不构成误导。
chineseSummary: FTC 法第 5 条禁止不公平或欺骗性的商业行为，是联邦层面的消费者保护基线，涵盖广告、定价与会员/订阅条款。
englishSummary: FTC Act Section 5 prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices — the federal consumer-protection baseline covering advertising, pricing and subscription/membership terms.

regulationName: NY Credit Card Surcharge Disclosure (GBL §518)
chineseTitle: 纽约州信用卡附加费披露法（双重定价）
englishTitle: NY Credit Card Surcharge Disclosure
agency: NY Dept. of State / DCWP
applicabilityThreshold: 所有对刷卡加收附加费的纽约州商家
keyRequirements: 须先标示最高（信用卡）总价；附加费不得超过刷卡处理成本；结账时不得突然加价。
complaintEnforcementRecord: 每次违规最高 $500；由州务厅/DCWP 执法。
recommendedAction: 若 App/POS 加收刷卡附加费，确保事先展示信用卡总价并封顶至处理成本。
chineseSummary: 纽约州自 2024-02-11 起要求对刷卡加收附加费的商家先标示最高（信用卡）总价，附加费不得超过刷卡处理成本，且不得在结账时突然加价。对瑞幸 App/POS 至关重要：若加收附加费须事先全额展示信用卡价。
englishSummary: Since 2024-02-11, NY requires sellers imposing card surcharges to post the highest (credit) total price, cap the surcharge at processing cost, and not add a surprise surcharge at checkout. Critical for Luckin's app/POS — if surcharging, show the full credit price up front.

regulationName: ADA Title III — Website/App Accessibility (Public Accommodation)
chineseTitle: ADA 第三章（网站/App 无障碍）
englishTitle: ADA Title III — Website/App Accessibility
agency: US DOJ (private suits)
applicabilityThreshold: 公共场所（含餐饮点单网站/App）
keyRequirements: 点单网站/App 须无障碍；法院普遍以 WCAG 2.1 AA 为事实标准。
complaintEnforcementRecord: Robles v. Domino's（第九巡回 2019）确认餐饮 App 须无障碍；每年逾 3,000 起网站无障碍诉讼。
recommendedAction: 对点单 App/网站进行 WCAG 2.1 AA 无障碍审计与整改。
chineseSummary: ADA 第三章经判例适用于公共场所的网站与 App。瑞幸以预点单 App/网站为核心，是无障碍诉讼的高暴露目标；法院普遍以 WCAG 2.1 AA 作为事实标准。Robles v. Domino's 确认餐饮点单 App 必须无障碍。
englishSummary: ADA Title III applies via case law to public-accommodation websites and apps. Luckin's order-ahead app/site is a high-exposure litigation target; courts apply WCAG 2.1 AA as the de facto standard. Robles v. Domino's confirmed a restaurant ordering app must be accessible.

regulationName: NY Automatic Renewal Law (GBL §527-a)
chineseTitle: 纽约州自动续费法
englishTitle: NY Automatic Renewal Law
agency: NY AG / DCWP
applicabilityThreshold: 提供自动续费消费计划的商家
keyRequirements: 清晰显著披露自动续费；取得明确同意；提供便捷的线上取消；续费提醒。
complaintEnforcementRecord: 由纽约州检察长就消费者保护提起执法。
recommendedAction: 如有自动续费会员，落实显著披露、明确同意与一键取消。
chineseSummary: 纽约州自动续费法要求对自动续费的消费计划清晰显著披露条款、取得明确同意、提供便捷线上取消并发送续费提醒。若瑞幸推出付费自动续费会员/订阅则适用。
englishSummary: NY's Automatic Renewal Law requires clear/conspicuous disclosure, affirmative consent, easy online cancellation and renewal reminders for auto-renewing consumer plans. Applies if Luckin offers a paid auto-renewing membership/subscription.

regulationName: NY Gift Card Rules (GBL §396-i)
chineseTitle: 纽约州礼品卡规则
englishTitle: NY Gift Card Rules
agency: NY DCP / AG
applicabilityThreshold: 销售礼品卡/礼券的商家
keyRequirements: 最低 9 年有效期（2022-12-10 起发行）；不得收取不活跃/服务费；余额 <$5 可兑现。
complaintEnforcementRecord: 由纽约州检察长执法。
recommendedAction: 如销售礼品卡，确认有效期 ≥9 年、无不活跃费并支持小额兑现。
chineseSummary: 纽约州礼品卡规则要求 2022-12-10 起发行的礼品卡至少 9 年有效、不得收取不活跃或服务费，余额低于 5 美元可要求兑现。若瑞幸销售礼品卡/储值，须遵守上述条款。
englishSummary: NY's gift-card rules require a minimum 9-year term (cards issued on/after 2022-12-10), no inactivity/service fees, and cash-out of balances under $5. If Luckin sells gift cards/stored value, these terms apply.

regulationName: NY SHIELD Act (Data Security & Breach Notification)
chineseTitle: 纽约州 SHIELD 法（数据安全与泄露通知）
englishTitle: NY SHIELD Act
agency: NY AG
applicabilityThreshold: 持有纽约州居民私人信息的任何企业
keyRequirements: 采取合理数据安全措施；发生泄露须通知（2024 修订要求发现后 30 天内）。
complaintEnforcementRecord: 纽约州检察长就数据安全失职达成和解。
recommendedAction: 维护合理数据安全措施与泄露通知预案，覆盖 App 顾客数据。
chineseSummary: 纽约州 SHIELD 法要求持有纽约居民私人信息的企业采取合理数据安全措施，并在数据泄露时通知（2024 修订要求发现后 30 天内）。瑞幸 App 收集顾客 PII/支付数据，须维护安全措施与泄露应急预案。
englishSummary: NY's SHIELD Act requires businesses holding NY residents' private information to maintain reasonable data safeguards and to notify on a breach (30 days post-discovery per the 2024 amendment). Luckin's app collects customer PII/payment data, so safeguards and a breach plan are required.

regulationName: NYC Item Pricing & Price-Scanner Rules (Admin Code §20-708)
chineseTitle: NYC 单品定价与扫描器规则
englishTitle: NYC Item Pricing & Price-Scanner Rules
agency: NYC DCWP
applicabilityThreshold: 销售带标签商品的零售店（30 英尺内有扫描器可豁免）
keyRequirements: 单品标价或合规扫描器；多重标价按最低价销售。
complaintEnforcementRecord: DCWP 进行单品定价检查并罚款。
recommendedAction: 对店内包装零售商品落实单品标价或合规扫描器。
chineseSummary: NYC 要求零售店对带标签商品单品标价，或在 30 英尺内提供合规价格扫描器；同一商品有多重标价时须按最低价销售。适用于店内销售的袋装咖啡豆、瓶装饮品等包装零售商品。
englishSummary: NYC requires retail stores to item-price tagged goods or provide a qualifying price scanner within 30 ft; multi-priced items must be sold at the lowest price. Applies to packaged retail goods sold in-store (bagged beans, bottled drinks).

regulationName: NYC Weights & Measures Device Inspection
chineseTitle: NYC 度量衡器具检验
englishTitle: NYC Weights & Measures Device Inspection
agency: NYC DCWP
applicabilityThreshold: 使用商业称重/计量器具的企业（仅当按重量/计量销售时）
keyRequirements: DCWP 年度称具检验；器具须准确；每台 $20–$100。
complaintEnforcementRecord: DCWP 查封不准确的称具。
recommendedAction: 核实是否按重量销售；如有，安排 DCWP 称具年度检验。
chineseSummary: NYC 要求使用商业称重/计量器具的企业接受 DCWP 年度检验并保持器具准确。仅当瑞幸在 POS 按重量销售（如按重量计价的咖啡豆）时触发；固定价格饮品不适用。
englishSummary: NYC requires businesses using commercial weighing/measuring devices to pass annual DCWP inspection and keep devices accurate. Triggered only if Luckin sells by weight at POS (e.g., beans priced by weight); not for fixed-price drinks.

regulationName: FTC Negative Option / "Click-to-Cancel" Rule
chineseTitle: FTC 负面选项/“一键取消”规则（已被撤销）
englishTitle: FTC Click-to-Cancel Rule
agency: FTC
applicabilityThreshold: 提供订阅/自动续费的商家
keyRequirements: 规则已被第八巡回法院于 2025-07-08 撤销（程序原因）；FTC 重启规则制定，ROSCA 执法继续。
complaintEnforcementRecord: 第八巡回法院 2025 年 7 月撤销该规则。
recommendedAction: 持续关注 FTC 订阅规则重启；如有订阅业务，遵循 ROSCA 与州法。
chineseSummary: FTC 的负面选项/“一键取消”规则已于 2025-07-08 被第八巡回法院以程序原因撤销，目前不具约束力；FTC 正重启规则制定，ROSCA 执法继续。若瑞幸推出付费订阅/会员，应持续关注。
englishSummary: The FTC's Negative Option / Click-to-Cancel rule was vacated by the 8th Circuit on 2025-07-08 (procedural) and is not currently binding; the FTC is restarting rulemaking and ROSCA enforcement continues. Monitor if Luckin launches a paid subscription/membership.

regulationName: NYC DCWP Consumer Complaint Process
chineseTitle: NYC DCWP 消费者投诉处理机制
englishTitle: NYC DCWP Consumer Complaint Process
agency: NYC DCWP
applicabilityThreshold: 所有可能被投诉的 NYC 企业
keyRequirements: DCWP 受理/调解消费者投诉；企业须回应问询/检查。
complaintEnforcementRecord: DCWP 的调解与裁决是常规流程。
recommendedAction: 建立 DCWP 投诉响应流程，及时回应并整改。
chineseSummary: NYC DCWP 受理并调解消费者投诉，企业须回应 DCWP 的问询与检查。瑞幸将收到 DCWP 转来的投诉，须及时回应并整改以避免升级。
englishSummary: NYC DCWP receives and mediates consumer complaints; businesses must respond to inquiries/inspections. Luckin will receive DCWP complaint forwards and must respond and cure to avoid escalation.
